reports.what_it_is: An open-source Lean 4 library of formalized STATEMENTS (not proofs) of mathematical conjectures, built on Mathlib and run by Google DeepMind (maintainer/merger of record: mo271 = Moritz Firsching, first author of the companion paper arXiv:2605.13171, "Formal Conjectures: An Open and Evolving Benchmark for Verified Discovery in Mathematics", May 2026). It doubles as an AI benchmark ("verified discovery"): frozen immutable benchmark snapshots are tagged (currently bench-v1-lean4.27.0). Very alive: 1,473 merged PRs total, 144 merged in June 2026 alone (~5/day), 677 open issues, 1,026 stars, active Zulip channel (leanprover Zulip #Formal-conjectures, channel 524981). Scale as of 2026-07-01: 819 .lean files, 3,071 attribute-tagged statements in the live JSON dump (1,156 "research open", 1,051 "research solved", 529 test, 183 API, 152 textbook); repo HEAD greps slightly higher (1,167 open / 1,066 solved); 3,134 sorries.
reports.data_model: The atomic entity is a Lean theorem/def with mandatory attributes; the FULLY-QUALIFIED LEAN NAME is the de-facto stable ID. Examples: `CollatzConjecture.collatz_conjecture` (module FormalConjectures.Wikipedia.CollatzConjecture, `@[category research open, AMS 11 37]`), `Erdos1.erdos_1` plus variant `erdos_1.variants.weaker` (`@[category textbook, AMS 5 11]`), `OeisA228828.a_zero` (`@[category test, AMS 11]`), namespace `Arxiv.«0912.2382»`. Directory = source: FormalConjectures/{ErdosProblems (473 files, named N.lean = erdosproblems.com/N), Wikipedia (133 files), GreensOpenProblems (51), WrittenOnTheWallII (47), Paper (24), OEIS (21, named 228828.lean = OEIS A228828), Arxiv (14, dirs named by arXiv ID e.g. 0912.2382/), Mathoverflow (12, named by question ID e.g. 1973.lean), Books, Millenium, Kourovka, HilbertProblems, OpenQuantumProblems, OptimizationConstants, LittProblems, Subsets, Other}. Source attribution lives in the module docstring, e.g. Collatz: `*References:* * [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Collatz_conjecture) * [erdosproblems.com/1135](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1135)` + bibliography entries. Attributes (defined in FormalConjectures/Util/Attributes/Basic.lean): `@[category textbook|research open|research solved|test|API]` (exactly one required), `@[AMS n ...]` (>=1 MSC2020 top-level code required), optional `@[formal_proof using formal_conjectures|lean4|other_system at "url"]`, and an `answer(sorry)` elaborator for problems demanding an answer term. Solved = informal proof widely accepted by experts (formal proof NOT required). Machine-readable dump record fields: theorem, module, category, githubPath, githubUrl, sourceUrl, collection, collectionUrl, subjects [{code,name}], hasFormalProof, formalProofKind, formalProofLink; plus versoFragments.moduleDocs (rendered per-module reference HTML containing the actual Wikipedia/arXiv/OEIS URLs) and versoFragments.constLinks (4,425 decl -> site URL + docHtml). NO Wikidata QIDs anywhere (grep across all .lean and .md: zero hits).
reports.access: git clone https://github.com/google-deepmind/formal-conjectures (Lean 4 v4.27.0 toolchain, lake + mathlib cache). Machine-readable JSON dump served from the docs site: https://google-deepmind.github.io/formal-conjectures/data/conjectures.json (3,071 records + stats + versoFragments; regenerated by CI via `lake exe extract_names --exclude=statement,docstring,moduleDocstrings` on push to main; GitHub Pages serves access-control-allow-origin: * so it is fetchable from a Worker). Per-theorem web pages: https://google-deepmind.github.io/formal-conjectures/theorem/?name=CollatzConjecture.collatz_conjecture (200 OK, name param = fully-qualified decl). doc-gen4 API docs under /doc/, e.g. .../doc/FormalConjectures/Wikipedia/CollatzConjecture.html (200 OK). Immutable git tags: bench-v1-lean4.27.0 (benchmark snapshots, fixes go to v{N+1}, never patched) and monthly v4.X.Y tags tracking Mathlib releases. LICENSE: Apache-2.0 for code; About page states other materials are CC-BY 4.0. LeanExplore indexes FormalConjectures as one of 9 packages (api/v2, https://www.leanexplore.com/api/v2, API-key required — Jack's key live-verified 2026-06-23 per local memory; note leanexplore.com Cloudflare 403s default Python UA). No rate limits documented on the Pages endpoints.
reports.existing_crosslinks: (1) Wikipedia: 152 .lean files cite en.wikipedia.org, 167 unique article URLs (e.g. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Collatz_conjecture in Wikipedia/CollatzConjecture.lean; also Wikipedia URLs appear in Erdos/other files). The 'wikipedia' GitHub label is applied automatically by .github/labeler.yml to any PR touching FormalConjectures/Wikipedia/** — 365 such PRs all-time; workflow is ordinary community PRs (authors like henrykmichalewski, ChakshuGupta13, rwst, YanYablonovskiy) human-reviewed and merged almost always by mo271. (2) erdosproblems.com — a real BIDIRECTIONAL sync: .github/workflows/update_erdosproblems.yml fires a repository_dispatch (event formal-conjecture-updated) to teorth/erdosproblems on every push to main, and scripts/check_erdos_status.py pulls teorth/erdosproblems data/problems.yaml and diffs each `theorem erdos_N`'s @[category research open|solved] against the site's status (states incl. "solved (Lean)"), auto-filing issues on mismatch; erdosproblems.com's problems.yaml records carry `formalized: {state: yes/no}` and `oeis: [A276661, ...]` arrays. (3) OEIS: A-numbers as filenames + reference links. (4) arXiv: IDs as directory names + abs links; the repo's own paper is arXiv:2605.13171. (5) MathOverflow question IDs as filenames with question+asker links. (6) Mathlib: built on it, tracks monthly releases, FormalConjecturesForMathlib/ stages upstreamable defs; @[formal_proof using lean4 at "url"] links to external Lean proofs (181 formal_proof occurrences). (7) LeanExplore indexes the whole library. NO Wikidata links exist today.

linkage_opportunities.join_key: en.wikipedia.org article URL in versoFragments.moduleDocs (per-module references HTML) -> Wikipedia title -> Wikidata QID via sitelink; carry the FC fully-qualified decl name as the payload
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Nightly fetch of https://google-deepmind.github.io/formal-conjectures/data/conjectures.json; parse moduleDocs hrefs for en.wikipedia.org; resolve title->QID with MediaWiki pageprops (wikipedia-search/wikidata-search skills); emit catalog/data JSONL rows {fc_decl, module, category, ams[], qid, wp_title} and render as 'stated-but-unproved' frontier nodes in concept_graph.json
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Three checks per link: (a) follow Wikipedia redirects to the canonical title and require the QID's enwiki sitelink to round-trip to that title; (b) require topical consistency: the FC @[AMS] code must be compatible with the article's subject (e.g. AMS 11 vs a number-theory article) — mismatches go to a human review queue, not the graph; (c) re-verify each nightly against a fresh conjectures.json so category drift (open->solved) and file moves are caught; pin the FC git commit SHA (or bench tag) in the row for provenance

linkage_opportunities.join_key: fully-qualified FC decl name (e.g. CollatzConjecture.collatz_conjecture)
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Extend the existing GET /decl/:name (currently Mathlib-only) with a FormalConjectures namespace: if the name is in the cached conjectures.json index, 302 to https://google-deepmind.github.io/formal-conjectures/theorem/?name=<decl> (or /doc/ page)
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Resolver only redirects on exact membership in the current conjectures.json 'theorem' field (3,071-entry set) — never string-guessing; 404 otherwise; cache is dated and re-pulled nightly so renamed/deleted decls stop resolving instead of dangling

linkage_opportunities.to: Erdős problems triangle (FC <-> erdosproblems.com <-> OEIS <-> Wikidata)
linkage_opportunities.join_key: erdos problem number N: FC theorem name regex erdos_(\d+) <-> teorth/erdosproblems problems.yaml 'number' <-> its 'oeis' array <-> Wikidata P829 (OEIS ID)
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Reuse FC's own join logic (scripts/check_erdos_status.py regex CATEGORY_THEN_THEOREM) to map decl->N; join problems.yaml for status/prize/oeis; attach to WikiLean QID nodes via OEIS-ID statements on Wikidata
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Consistency vote across three independent sources: FC @[category] vs problems.yaml status.state (FC's CI already files issues on mismatch — inherit that signal rather than trusting either side alone); OEIS A-number must appear both in problems.yaml and on the Wikidata item (P829) before an edge is drawn

linkage_opportunities.join_key: @[formal_proof using lean4 at "url"] URLs when they point into leanprover-community/mathlib4, plus FormalConjecturesForMathlib upstreaming PRs
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Parse formalProofKind/formalProofLink from conjectures.json; where kind=lean4 and URL is a Mathlib blob/commit, resolve the target decl and link the WikiLean node to BOTH the FC statement and the Mathlib proof
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Verify the linked decl actually exists via WikiLean's existing Mathlib name index / mathlib-search before storing; keep the FC commit link as evidence; 'hasFormalProof:false' entries must render as statement-only

linkage_opportunities.join_key: fully-qualified decl name within package=FormalConjectures
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Use api/v2 search with --packages FormalConjectures as a secondary resolver/enricher (descriptions, source links) for FC decls surfaced on WikiLean pages
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Exact-name match against conjectures.json first; LeanExplore results only accepted when the returned fully-qualified name is byte-identical (semantic hits are suggestions, never auto-links); requires LEANEXPLORE_API_KEY

wikilean_hooks: FRONTIER LAYER: WikiLean's formalization-status vocabulary gains a middle tier — 'statement formalized in formal-conjectures, proof open' — between 'not formalized' and 'proved in Mathlib'. 545 Wikipedia-collection statements (133 files, 167 unique enwiki URLs) map almost directly onto WikiLean article slugs (Collatz_conjecture, Abc_conjecture, Beal_conjecture...); many are articles WikiLean already mirrors.
RESOLVER: add a FormalConjectures source to the live GET /decl/:name endpoint keyed off the public conjectures.json dump (CORS *, fetchable straight from the Worker; cache in KV, refresh nightly).

CONCEPT GRAPH: add QID-keyed conjecture nodes with category/AMS metadata from the dump; the @[AMS] MSC2020 codes give a ready-made subject clustering that complements graph centrality; 'research solved' + formal_proof links give resolution events to animate the frontier over time.

ANNOTATION PIPELINE: annotations on conjecture statements can cite the FC decl as evidence with pinned provenance (FC commit SHA or immutable bench-v{N} tag), matching WikiLean's revid-pinned stale-but-consistent model — both systems version-pin deliberately.

DRIFT MONITORING (mirrors the mission's op #3): nightly diff of conjectures.json category fields catches open->solved transitions (e.g. PR #4103 'mark variant as solved'); flag affected WikiLean pages for re-review — exactly analogous to FC's own check_erdos_status.py issue-filer, which is also a reusable design pattern for WikiLean<->upstream status sync.

UPSTREAM PLAY: FC has no Wikidata field and an active community (CLA + human review, AGENTS.md explicitly welcomes AI agents). WikiLean can host the FC-decl->QID mapping table itself first (catalog/data JSONL), then propose upstream either a docstring reference-line convention ([Wikidata](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q837314)) or reuse of the proposed 'Mathlib declaration'-style Wikidata property with FC decls — the Zulip channel (leanprover #Formal-conjectures) is the venue; note attribute changes do land (open PR #4368 adds @[proof_condition]).
reports.quality_mechanism: Layered and unusually strong for a statements-only corpus: (1) every PR needs a Google CLA and human maintainer review (mo271 reviews/merges nearly everything; second reviewers like franzhusch appear); issue-first workflow with milestones and AMS-classified labels. (2) CI: full `lake build` against pinned Mathlib release, custom linters (e.g. ModuleDocstringLinter), copyright-header check; every statement MUST carry exactly one @[category] and >=1 @[AMS] tag or it fails. (3) Adversarial AI auditing: README commits to 'careful human review of contributions, and plan to periodically leverage AlphaProof to help identify potential misformalisations'; the paper states 'AI-generated proofs and disproofs serve as a valuable auditing mechanism' (a trivially-provable 'open conjecture' is a detected misformalization). (4) Status ground-truthing: check_erdos_status.py cross-validates @[category] against erdosproblems.com's problems.yaml and auto-files mismatch issues; repository_dispatch pushes updates back. (5) Benchmark integrity: bench tags are immutable — misformalization fixes are never patched into an existing benchmark version, only into v{N+1}. (6) Community telemetry: Giscus (GitHub Discussions) comments/votes on every site page; 'fix misformalisation' PRs are an explicit, welcomed contribution class (e.g. #4199 fix IsCarmichael, #4096 exclude n=0, #4224 add Nonempty).
key_facts: No Wikidata QIDs exist anywhere in the repo (zero grep hits in .lean/.md) — the FC-decl->QID mapping is genuinely unclaimed territory, and WikiLean's QID-keyed catalog is positioned to own it.

The fully-qualified Lean name is the ONLY per-conjecture stable ID; it is name-stable in practice but not guaranteed (renames happen in fix PRs), so any external mapping must pin a git SHA or bench tag alongside the name.

A public machine-readable dump already exists and is CORS-open: google-deepmind.github.io/formal-conjectures/data/conjectures.json — 3,071 records with name/module/category/AMS/formal-proof fields PLUS versoFragments.moduleDocs, which contains the per-conjecture source URLs (the Wikipedia links) as rendered HTML. No scraping of .lean files needed.

The 'wikipedia' PR label is purely path-based (labeler.yml glob on FormalConjectures/Wikipedia/**), not a semantic workflow — 365 such PRs are ordinary community contributions reviewed by DeepMind maintainers, mostly mo271.

The repo already operates a production-grade cross-database status sync with erdosproblems.com (repository_dispatch out on every main push; check_erdos_status.py in, diffing @[category] against teorth/erdosproblems problems.yaml and auto-filing issues) — the exact pattern WikiLean's drift-monitoring mission needs, and proof the maintainers accept external join fabric.

'research solved' does NOT mean formally proved — it means an informal proof is widely accepted; formal proof existence is tracked separately via @[formal_proof using formal_conjectures|lean4|other_system at url] (181 occurrences). WikiLean must not render FC 'solved' as 'formalized proof exists'.

Benchmark snapshots are immutable git tags (bench-v1-lean4.27.0 is the only one so far); misformalization fixes bump v{N+1}, never patch v{N} — same stale-but-consistent philosophy as WikiLean's revid pinning.

Scale and composition skew heavily Erdős: 1,577 of 3,071 statements are Erdős Problems vs 545 Wikipedia-collection; only ~1,156 are open research conjectures; 529 are 'test' sanity checks and 183 'API' statements that a frontier-map ingest should filter out by @[category].

Dedicated AGENTS.md welcomes AI-agent contributions with explicit conventions, and CONTRIBUTING caps included proofs at 25-50 lines (longer proofs must be hosted externally and linked via formal_proof).
Licensing is clean for reuse: Apache-2.0 code, CC-BY 4.0 other materials; contribution requires the Google CLA.

The companion paper (arXiv:2605.13171, May 2026, Firsching/Lezeau/Mercuri/.../Agüera y Arcas/Kohli) reports the benchmark 'has already been leveraged to make new mathematical discoveries, including the resolution of open research conjectures'.

LeanExplore already indexes FormalConjectures as one of 9 packages via api/v2 (verified against Jack's local lean-explore skill, live-tested 2026-06-23), giving a second, semantic resolver for FC decls.
open_questions: Would FC maintainers accept Wikidata QID reference lines in module docstrings (or a @[wikidata] -style attribute)? No precedent or discussion found; needs a Zulip/issue ask — the attribute system is demonstrably extensible (open PR #4368 adds @[proof_condition]).

Exact freshness contract of conjectures.json: it regenerates on push to main via CI, but the workflow has a website-only fallback that curls the LIVE json back — worst-case staleness is unquantified; commit 47f1df0 just added dates to extract_names, so date fields may appear in the dump soon.

conjectures.json is generated with --exclude=statement,docstring,moduleDocstrings, so pretty-printed Lean statement text is NOT in the public dump (docstring HTML is, via versoFragments) — getting full statements requires running `lake exe extract_names` locally against a checkout.

The precise membership of benchmark bench-v1-lean4.27.0 vs current main (which problems were frozen) requires checking out the tag; FC100OpenSet1/FC100SolvedSet1 subset files exist (seeded random samples, SEED=42) but their role in official evals is only documented in the paper, which I read only via abstract.

Some Wikipedia-collection files cite multiple enwiki articles (167 unique URLs across 152 citing files, 545 statements) — a canonicalization rule (first Reference wins? per-theorem overrides?) must be decided and spot-checked by a human before bulk QID assignment.

LeanExplore api/v2 requires an API key (Cloudflare also blocks default Python UA with error 1010) — fine for Jack locally, but a WikiLean Worker integration would need the key as a Worker secret and its rate limits are undocumented.

reports.source: LMFDB — the L-functions and modular forms database (https://www.lmfdb.org, staging at https://beta.lmfdb.org)
reports.what_it_is: LMFDB is the reference database of number-theoretic objects — L-functions, classical/Hilbert/Siegel modular forms, elliptic curves, number fields, p-adic fields, abelian varieties, abstract groups — stored in a 620-table PostgreSQL database with tens of millions of rows per major table, plus a 1,716-knowl human-written "knowledge" layer of definitions. It is run by "The LMFDB Collaboration" with 4 managing editors (John Jones/ASU, Jennifer Paulhus/Mount Holyoke, David Roe/MIT, Andrew Sutherland/MIT) and 18 associate editors, funded by NSF, EPSRC and the Simons Foundation. It is very alive: 31 commits to LMFDB/lmfdb in June 2026 alone, and a Renaissance-Philanthropy-funded Mathlib-linking project (LeanBridge, Birkbeck/Roe/Sutherland) actively committing as of 2026-06-29. Crucially for WikiLean, a systematic LMFDB↔Mathlib linking effort is ALREADY underway using a first-class `mathlib=` keyword in LMFDB's knowl markup.
reports.data_model: TWO id systems. (1) OBJECT LABELS — human-readable, per-type canonical labels, verified live via the API: elliptic curve over Q `11.a2` (= conductor 11, isogeny class a, curve 2; API record shows ainvs [0,-1,1,-10,-20], rank 0, torsion 5, and the DISTINCT legacy Cremona label `Clabel: 11a1` in the same row); number field `2.0.4.1` = Q(i) with format d.r.D.i (degree, real embeddings, |discriminant|, disambiguating index — quoted from knowl nf.label); classical modular form `1.12.a.a` (level.weight.character-orbit.newform-orbit, e.g. the Delta function at /ModularForm/GL2/Q/holomorphic/1/12/a/a). Objects live in prefix-grouped tables: ec_curvedata (3.82M rows), nf_fields (22.2M), mf_newforms (1.14M), lfunc_lfunctions (24.2M). (2) KNOWL IDs — dot-namespaced concept identifiers like `nf.degree`, `ec.q.torsion_subgroup`, `group.abelian` in table `kwl_knowls` with fields id, cat, title, content, authors, status, timestamp, plus TWO structured cross-linking columns: `links` (knowl ids referenced via {{KNOWL(...)}}) and `defines` (terms defined — e.g. nf.degree_mathlib_def has defines=['module rank']). Knowls are versioned append-only (current version = DISTINCT ON (id) ... ORDER BY timestamp DESC). Content is markdown+macros: {{KNOWL('nf','number field')}} for internal links, {{DEFINES("term", mathlib=..., wikidata=..., wikipedia=..., nlab=..., mathworld=..., stacks=..., groupprops=..., zbl=..., mr=..., arxiv=..., doi=..., href=...)}} for external definition links, and \cite{doi:...,MR:...,arXiv:...} for citations (same keys supported).
reports.access: (a) JSON API: https://www.lmfdb.org/api/ — per-table queries like https://www.lmfdb.org/api/ec_curvedata/?lmfdb_label=11.a2&_format=json&_fields=lmfdb_label,ainvs,conductor (verified working); type-prefixed values (i=int, s=string, li=list-of-ints...), meta-params _format (json/yaml/html), _fields, _sort, _offset; hard limits: 100 rows/page, ~10k/query; no documented rate limit ("responsible use"). No /api2 (404 on both www and beta). GOTCHA: the site now has a JS bot-gate (recaptcha); programmatic access works by sending header `Cookie: human=1`. (b) BULK: public read-only PostgreSQL mirror at devmirror.lmfdb.xyz:5432, dbname=lmfdb, user=lmfdb, password=lmfdb (credentials published openly in LeanBridge's update_knowls.py) — I connected live and ran SQL, including on kwl_knowls, which is NOT exposed via the /api route. (c) Knowl rendering: https://www.lmfdb.org/knowledge/show/{id}; page source embeds full edit history with raw macro source. (d) LICENSE (from https://beta.lmfdb.org/license, quoted): "The database underpinning the LMFDB is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License (CC-BY-SA)"; website code GPLv2 (repo LICENSE says GPLv2+). Note share-alike: compatible with Wikipedia's CC BY-SA 4.0, viral for derived databases. (e) Community: lmfdb-support@googlegroups.com, Zulip at lmfdb.zulipchat.com, GitHub LMFDB/lmfdb (289 stars).
reports.existing_crosslinks: MATHLIB (the headline): 62 of 1,716 live knowls carry `mathlib=` links (my SQL count on devmirror), across categories af, ag, alg, character (Dirichlet), ec (30 knowls!), group, lf, nf, ring. Exact syntax from nf.degree_mathlib_def raw source: `This is also defined in mathlib as \`finrank ℚ K\`, where $K$ is the number field and \`finrank ℚ\` is its {{DEFINES("module rank", mathlib="LinearAlgebra/Dimension/Finrank.html#Module.finrank")}} over the rationals.` — the xid is a mathlib4_docs path where the URL fragment after `#` IS the fully-qualified Mathlib decl name (Module.finrank); knowl.py expands it to https://leanprover-community.github.io/mathlib4_docs/Mathlib/{xid} with display text "mathlib:Module.finrank". Another: nf.class_number → NumberTheory/NumberField/ClassNumber.html#NumberField.classNumber. Three dedicated knowls exist (nf.degree_mathlib_def, group.group_mathlib_defn, ring.field_of_fractions_mathlib), all authored 2025-10-23 by Chris Birkbeck, all still "awaiting review"; nf.degree_mathlib_def edited 2026-03-20 by David Kurniadi Angdinata. Theorem-level `\cite{mathlib:...}` is documented as supported but has ZERO uses so far (SQL: 0 knowls contain 'mathlib:'). The systematic effort is github.com/CBirkbeck/LeanBridge (Apache-2.0, created 2025-07-29, contributors CBirkbeck, roed314=David Roe, pitmonticone), a Verso blueprint auto-generated from the knowl DB by blueprint/src/update_knowls.py, which marks a knowl \leanok only when every DEFINES in it has a mathlib= link; funded via Renaissance Philanthropy's "Scalable theorem proving via mathematical databases" (Birkbeck/UEA + Roe + Sutherland/MIT). WIKIDATA: property P12987 "LMFDB knowl ID" (created 2024-09-11, formatter https://www.lmfdb.org/knowledge/show/$1) with 109 uses — e.g. Q83478 (group)→`group`, Q161172 (ring)→`ring`, Q870797 (modular form)→`mf.about`, Q181296 (abelian group)→`group.abelian` (the documented DEFINES example). NO Wikidata property exists for LMFDB OBJECT labels. Reverse direction is thin: only 2 knowls use wikidata=, 12 link wikipedia. Other DEFINES targets: nLab, MathWorld, Stacks project tags, groupprops, zbMATH, MathSciNet, arXiv, DOI. NO OEIS crosslinks (0 knowl mentions). Wikipedia→LMFDB: only 15 en.wiki pages contain lmfdb.org; no Template:LMFDB.

linkage_opportunities.join_key: fully-qualified Mathlib decl name = URL fragment of the DEFINES mathlib= xid (e.g. 'LinearAlgebra/Dimension/Finrank.html#Module.finrank' → Module.finrank)

WHERE content LIKE '%mathlib=%'), regex-extract the mathlib= xids exactly as LeanBridge's update_knowls.py does; yields (knowl_id → decl_name) pairs — 62 today, growing under funded LeanBridge effort
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Resolve each decl through WikiLean's live GET /decl/:name against the Mathlib checkout (decl must exist, non-deprecated); HTTP-check the mathlib4_docs anchor resolves; flag pairs whose knowl was edited since last harvest (timestamp column) for re-review; track LMFDB review status (status=1 vs 0) as a provenance tier — all 62 are currently status 0 (awaiting review), so label them 'unreviewed upstream'

linkage_opportunities.join_key: QID ↔ LMFDB knowl id via Wikidata property P12987 (109 statements)
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: SPARQL pull of all P12987 statements; attach knowl ids as a crosslink field on WikiLean concept-graph nodes; conversely propose new P12987 statements for WikiLean's already-mapped QIDs whose concept has an obvious knowl (nf.class_number ↔ Q217413 class number, etc.)
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Round-trip: knowl must exist and be live (kwl_knowls status>=0, or HTTP 200 with non-'Page Not Found' title); string-similarity check knowl title vs QID English label/aliases; where the knowl itself carries DEFINES wikidata=/wikipedia= kwargs (2 and 12 knowls resp.), require agreement with P12987's inverse — disagreement = flag for human; new Wikidata statements go through Jack's human review per standing policy

linkage_opportunities.join_key: the triangle QID —P12987→ knowl —mathlib=→ decl vs QID —@[wikidata] tag/catalog→ decl
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: WikiLean's catalog and the @[wikidata] pipeline already map QID→decl; LMFDB now independently maps knowl→decl and Wikidata maps QID→knowl; compute both paths and diff
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Agreement of two independently-curated paths is strong evidence a QID↔decl mapping is correct; disagreement is a concrete, human-reviewable error report usable in BOTH directions (fix WikiLean's tag, or propose a knowl correction upstream via the LeanBridge workflow — Roe/Sutherland gate knowl accounts)

linkage_opportunities.to: LMFDB objects (labels) from Wikipedia/Wikidata articles about famous objects

linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Propose a Wikidata 'LMFDB object label' property (or per-type properties) with formatter https://www.lmfdb.org/EllipticCurve/Q/$1 etc.; seed from famous objects (Gaussian integers→2.0.4.1, Ramanujan tau/Δ→1.12.a.a, X_0(11)→11.a); Jack already has an in-flight Wikidata property proposal (Mathlib declaration) — same playbook

linkage_opportunities.join_key: knowl id; edges from the kwl_knowls structured 'links' column and {{KNOWL(...)}} macro extraction
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Import LMFDB's internal knowl-reference graph (the same graph LeanBridge builds with networkx) as a specialist number-theory subgraph, joined to WikiLean nodes wherever P12987 or title-matching gives a QID
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Edge extraction is deterministic (regex identical to LMFDB's own KNOWL_RE) and reproducible against the versioned kwl_knowls table; joins to QIDs only via verified P12987 statements or human-approved matches, never fuzzy-only

wikilean_hooks: Direct SQL harvest job: connect to devmirror.lmfdb.xyz:5432 (lmfdb/lmfdb, public) and pull the 62 mathlib=-bearing knowls; store (knowl_id, decl_name, knowl_timestamp, status) in the catalog; no scraping needed and D1 stays canonical for WikiLean's own data

GET /decl/:name reverse citation index: add 'Cited by LMFDB knowl nf.class_number' entries — exactly parallel to the existing Wikidata-property formatter-URL integration; the decl name is literally the anchor fragment of the knowl's mathlib= xid

Concept-graph nodes: attach lmfdb_knowl ids via P12987 (109 QIDs already done on Wikidata's side) and render an LMFDB chip on article pages whose QID has one — e.g. the Modular form article (Q870797 → mf.about)

@[wikidata] pipeline synergy: WikiLean's QID→decl mappings are exactly the input LeanBridge needs to add DEFINES mathlib= kwargs to more knowls; contribute them upstream via LeanBridge PRs (knowl accounts gated by David Roe / Andrew Sutherland on lmfdb.zulipchat.com or leanprover.zulipchat.com) — a human-gated upstream path structurally identical to the existing mathlib4 tagging-bot workflow
Nightly triangle-consistency check (QID→knowl→decl vs QID→decl) as a new manage/ worklist source — disagreements become review items in the propose-then-approve queue

Annotation model kinship: knowls are LMFDB's version of WikiLean annotations (dot-namespaced ids, versioned edits, authorship, reviewed/beta status, staging site) — their 'awaiting review'/'reviewed' status field maps cleanly onto WikiLean's provenance tiers when importing; import knowl content only with CC BY-SA 4.0 attribution and share-alike compliance

Wikidata property proposal #2: 'LMFDB object label' — Jack's Mathlib-declaration proposal experience applies directly, and P12987's existence (knowl-level) is precedent that Wikidata accepts LMFDB identifiers
key_facts: A funded, active LMFDB↔Mathlib linking project already exists: LeanBridge (github.com/CBirkbeck/LeanBridge, created 2025-07-29, last commit 2026-06-29), by Chris Birkbeck (UEA), David Roe (MIT, LMFDB managing editor), Andrew Sutherland (MIT), funded via Renaissance Philanthropy's 'Scalable theorem proving via mathematical databases' — WikiLean should coordinate, not duplicate

The Mathlib join key is machine-perfect: DEFINES mathlib= xids embed the fully-qualified decl name as the mathlib4_docs URL fragment (e.g. #Module.finrank, #NumberField.classNumber), directly resolvable by WikiLean's /decl/:name

Bulk access is trivially open: public read-only Postgres at devmirror.lmfdb.xyz:5432 with user/password lmfdb/lmfdb (published in LeanBridge's own script); 620 tables; kwl_knowls (the knowl base) is queryable there but NOT via the JSON /api

Exact live counts (my SQL, 2026-07-01): 1,716 live knowls = 814 reviewed + 902 beta; 62 contain mathlib= links; 0 contain theorem-level mathlib citations; 2 link wikidata=; 12 link wikipedia; 0 mention OEIS
All mathlib-linking knowl edits date from 2025-10-23 (Birkbeck) onward and every one is still status 'awaiting review' — the LMFDB editorial review of the Mathlib links has not happened yet

Wikidata already has P12987 'LMFDB knowl ID' (since 2024-09-11, 109 items, formatter https://www.lmfdb.org/knowledge/show/$1) but NO property for LMFDB object labels; en.wikipedia has no LMFDB template and only ~15 pages citing lmfdb.org — object-level linking is wide open
Data license is CC BY-SA 4.0 (share-alike — same family as Wikipedia, so knowl-content reuse in WikiLean is license-compatible with attribution); site code GPLv2+
LMFDB's site now has a JS/recaptcha bot gate; automated fetches need Cookie: human=1 (or the DB mirror)
One LMFDB object carries TWO label systems (lmfdb_label 11.a2 vs Cremona Clabel 11a1) — any object-level crosslink must say which; the JSON API returns both in one record
The knowl table has structured 'links' and 'defines' columns — LMFDB maintains its own concept dependency graph, which LeanBridge already converts to a networkx graph / Verso blueprint
reports.quality_mechanism: Layered human review, closely analogous to WikiLean's moderation model. (1) Knowls have an explicit status lifecycle hard-coded in knowl.py: {'reviewed':1, 'beta':0, 'in progress':-1, 'deleted':-2}; titles render as '(reviewed)' or '(awaiting review)'; every edit is an append-only versioned row with author + microsecond timestamp, and the full edit history (raw macro source per revision) is embedded in the page. Editing requires an account granted by the managing editors (contact Roe/Sutherland). (2) A staging site (beta.lmfdb.org) fronts production; users are told to check beta before filing bugs. (3) Governance: 4 managing editors + 18 associate editors (editorial board page), NSF/EPSRC/Simons funded. (4) Data provenance: a dedicated 'rcs' knowl category (147 knowls) documents Reliability/Completeness/Source per data section, and each object section has Source/Reliability pages (e.g. /EllipticCurve/Source). (5) Code path: GitHub PR review on LMFDB/lmfdb. For the Mathlib links specifically the anti-slop loop is: human-authored DEFINES edit → LMFDB knowl review (pending) → LeanBridge blueprint regeneration where \leanok is granted only when every defined term in a knowl has a mathlib link, keeping an explicit todo-frontier.
open_questions: When/whether the 62 mathlib-linked knowls will pass LMFDB editorial review (all still status=0); who is designated to review them and does review re-verify the decl names against current Mathlib (decls can be renamed/deprecated upstream)

Would LMFDB accept AI-assisted DEFINES mathlib= proposals (e.g. seeded from WikiLean's QID→decl catalog) under a human-gated account, and what volume is welcome — needs a conversation with David Roe / Andrew Sutherland on lmfdb.zulipchat.com (their Zulip likely requires an account; not inspected)
devmirror.lmfdb.xyz sync cadence and stability guarantees (it is presented as a dev mirror, not a formal API contract) — unknown without asking; also whether they mind sustained external SQL load

No Wikidata property for LMFDB OBJECT labels exists — would the Wikidata community accept one property with type-dependent formatter URLs, or require per-object-type properties (elliptic curve label, number field label, newform label)?

Theorem-level linking (\cite with a mathlib key) is documented in doc.knowl.guidelines but has zero uses — is the syntax finalized (mathlib: prefix inside \cite?) and is theorem linking part of LeanBridge's funded scope timeline?
Renaissance Philanthropy grant size/duration for the LeanBridge project is not published on the project page
Whether knowl ids are guaranteed immutable (143 knowls are status=deleted; LeanBridge's update script warns on deletions) — deletion/renaming policy affects long-term crosslink stability

reports.source: Lean library landscape beyond Mathlib: CSLib, lean-pool, merely-true, mathlib.org cross-ref infrastructure (@[stacks]/@[wikidata], 1000/100/undergrad.yaml), Physlib (ex-PhysLean), LeanExplore
reports.what_it_is: A tiered ecosystem of Lean 4 libraries spanning a deliberate review-bar spectrum. (1) CSLib (github.com/leanprover/cslib, cslib.io) — "Mathlib for computer science," a Renaissance Philanthropy program (sponsors incl. Amazon, Google DeepMind, Stanford CAR; whitepaper arXiv:2602.04846 by Barrett, Chaudhuri, Montesi, de Moura et al.), created 2025-06-17, 606 stars, 176 .lean files / 3,210 declarations, maintainer-reviewed, mathlib-style, actively committed (last commit 2026-07-02). (2) lean-pool (github.com/Vilin97/lean-pool) — a monorepo "between mathlib and merely-true" preserving 117 verified formalization projects (841,688 LOC, 52,300 decls) gated by deterministic linters + a published LLM-review rubric instead of human review; by Vasily Ilin + Justin Asher (the LeanExplore author), UW Lean Hackathon 2026. (3) merely-true (github.com/merely-true/merely-true) — Kim Morrison-maintained proposal repo for AI-generated math with NO human review: auto-merge on CI, approved-identity accounts only, and files that stop compiling at toolchain bumps are deleted from master (41 .lean files today). (4) mathlib.org itself carries the cross-reference precedent: @[stacks]/@[kerodon]/@[wikidata] attributes plus three curated YAML lists rendered at leanprover-community.github.io. (5) Physlib (formerly PhysLean, formerly HEPLean) — physics library, just renamed and moved to leanprover-community/physlib, 9,337 decls, with a lower-bar "PhyslibAlpha" tier that is explicitly unmaintained-on-breakage.
reports.data_model: Universal Lean identifier = (library, fully-qualified decl name) — the pair is REQUIRED because bare names don't identify a library: CSLib has 292 decls outside the Cslib.* namespace that extend Mathlib namespaces (e.g. `Language.IsRegular.finite_nerodeQuotient` lives in CSLib but in Mathlib's `Language` namespace), Physlib decl names carry no prefix at all (`ACCSystem`), and lean-pool projects keep original namespaces (`Biswal.Theorem1.genFun_is_polynomial`, bare `ABC`). Same concept, two names across libraries: Mathlib `DFA` (Mathlib.Computability.DFA) vs CSLib `Cslib.Automata.DA` (Cslib/Computability/Automata/DA/Basic.lean). CSLib decls by kind: 1,658 theorems, 871 defs, 61 inductives, 27 classes; by area: Foundations 998, Languages 838, Computability 578, Logics 575, Crypto 88, MachineLearning 70, Algorithms 56, Probability 7. lean-pool's LeanPool/projects.yml is the richest per-project schema in the ecosystem — per project: slug (stable id), title, summary, branch, entry_module, authors, source{title, authors, arxiv (e.g. "2604.25246"), github_repo}, license, status (all 117 = "verified"), main_declarations, main_results[{declaration, informal}], tags, msc (MSC2020 codes, 117/117 have them; 40/117 have arXiv ids). merely-true: free-form directory structure by design (MerelyTrue/Landau/*.lean etc.), no metadata schema. Mathlib YAML lists: docs/1000.yaml keyed by Wikidata QID (`Q11518: {title: Pythagorean theorem, decl: EuclideanGeometry.dist_sq_eq_dist_sq_add_dist_sq_iff_angle_eq_pi_div_two, authors: ...}`), docs/100.yaml keyed by Freek number, docs/undergrad.yaml keyed by French-agrégation topic strings — only 1000.yaml is QID-keyed. Doc-gen4 decl URL scheme (identical across ALL these libraries): <docs-root>/<Module/Path>.html#<FullyQualifiedDeclName>, e.g. https://api.cslib.io/docs/Cslib/Computability/Automata/DA/Basic.html#Cslib.Automata.DA, https://vilin97.github.io/lean-pool/LeanPool/RamanujanTauMissesPrimes/Solution.html#ABC, https://physlib.io/docs/Physlib/QFT/AnomalyCancellation/Basic.html#ACCSystem. LeanExplore SearchResult: {id:int (DB primary key), name, module, docstring, source_text, source_link (GitHub URL), dependencies (JSON array of decl names — decl-level dependency edges!), informalization (AI-generated NL)}.
reports.access: All Apache-2.0 (lean-pool vendored projects individually Apache-2.0 x90 or MIT x27, tracked per-project in projects.yml). Machine-readable decl ground truth: every doc-gen4 site serves declarations/declaration-data.bmp — JSON despite the .bmp name, served content-type image/bmp, with CORS `access-control-allow-origin: *`: CSLib https://api.cslib.io/docs/declarations/declaration-data.bmp (32.5MB, {declarations:{name:{docLink,kind}}, instances, modules}; includes all deps — filter docLink startswith "./Cslib/" → 3,210), lean-pool https://vilin97.github.io/lean-pool/declarations/declaration-data.bmp (58.8MB, 52,300 own), Physlib https://physlib.io/docs/declarations/declaration-data.bmp (65.9MB, 9,337 own, cache-control max-age=604800), Mathlib at leanprover-community.github.io/mathlib4_docs/declarations/declaration-data.bmp. YAML lists raw from github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/blob/master/docs/{1000,100,undergrad,overview}.yaml; rendered at leanprover-community.github.io/{1000,100,undergrad}.html (all verified 200). projects.yml raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Vilin97/lean-pool/main/LeanPool/projects.yml. LeanExplore: `pip install lean-explore`; hosted API https://www.leanexplore.com/api/v2 requires free API key from leanexplore.com (site returns 403 to plain fetch — SPA + key gate); local backend: `lean-explore data fetch` downloads the full dataset, no key; MCP server built in (`lean-explore mcp serve`); paper arXiv:2506.11085. Version pinning: CSLib and lean-pool tag releases per Lean toolchain (both currently v4.32.0-rc1; CSLib also v4.31.0, v4.30.0); lean-pool has Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20513444 + CITATION.cff; merely-true has ZERO tags (its proposed tag-before-toolchain-bump policy not yet exercised) and pins mathlib at release tag v4.29.0.
reports.existing_crosslinks: Mathlib → external databases via Mathlib/Tactic/CrossRefAttribute.lean (author Damiano Testa): `@[stacks TAG]`, `@[kerodon TAG]`, `@[wikidata QID]` — each appends a markdown link to the decl's docstring (so it flows into mathlib4_docs HTML automatically) and records (declName, database, tag, comment) in a persistent env extension queryable via #stacks_tags/#kerodon_tags/#wikidata_tags. Verified example, Mathlib/Topology/NoetherianSpace.lean:75: `@[stacks 0052 "(1)"]` / `instance NoetherianSpace.set [NoetherianSpace α] (s : Set α) : NoetherianSpace s`. Counts in checkout 0d3e25fd (2026-06-27): @[stacks] 523 occurrences across 235 files; @[wikidata] 84 attribute usages (e.g. `@[wikidata Q659746]` in Order/RelClasses.lean, `@[wikidata Q1816967]` on Urysohn's lemma). Reverse direction absent: stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/0052 shows NO Lean/Mathlib backlink (verified). Mathlib docs/1000.yaml → Wikidata QIDs + 1000-plus.github.io (and carries the TODO comment: "perhaps add a wikidata version of the stacks attribute, and generate this file automatically" — i.e. 1000.yaml may become generated FROM @[wikidata] tags). lean-pool projects.yml → arXiv (40 projects, e.g. arxiv: "2604.25246"), MSC2020 codes (117/117), original github_repo (117/117), decl names (main_declarations 117/117). LeanExplore → per-decl GitHub source_link + decl-level dependencies arrays across 9 libraries (Batteries, CSLib, FLT, FormalConjectures, Init, Lean, Mathlib, PhysLean, Std — note it still lists the pre-rename name "PhysLean"). merely-true: no cross-links at all. CSLib: references.bib for literature, no database attributes yet — but since CSLib requires mathlib (pinned rev d52d26fc2f36...), mathlib's @[wikidata] attribute is importable and usable in CSLib source TODAY with zero new infrastructure.

linkage_opportunities.join_key: Wikidata QID ↔ (library='cslib', fully-qualified decl name), e.g. QID for 'deterministic finite automaton' ↔ Cslib.Automata.DA alongside Mathlib's DFA
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Extend WikiLean catalog/annotations with formalizations:[{lib,decl,url}] where url = https://api.cslib.io/docs/<Module>.html#<decl>; longer term, upstream @[wikidata] tags into CSLib source — the attribute already elaborates there because CSLib depends on mathlib
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Exact-key lookup of the decl name in api.cslib.io/docs/declarations/declaration-data.bmp (CORS-open JSON; filter docLink startswith './Cslib/'), re-run nightly to catch renames; pin the CSLib release tag (e.g. v4.32.0-rc1) in the record; human review of concept-to-decl mapping per WikiLean's existing patrol flow before any tag is proposed upstream

linkage_opportunities.join_key: projects.yml slug + main_declarations decl names; secondary joins via source.arxiv (40/117 projects) and msc codes (117/117) to arXiv/zbMATH-side identifiers
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Ingest LeanPool/projects.yml (single YAML, stable raw URL) as a 'formalization projects' layer: map each project's headline theorem to a QID where a Wikidata item exists (e.g. named theorems/open problems), link decl URLs at vilin97.github.io/lean-pool/<Module>.html#<decl>
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: (a) decl exists in lean-pool's declaration-data.bmp; (b) arXiv id resolves and its title matches projects.yml source.title (string-similarity gate + human approve); (c) project status=='verified' and repo CI green at the pinned release tag; QID mapping goes through WikiLean's human-review queue, never auto-published

linkage_opportunities.join_key: (tag, file path, decl name) — ONLY tag- or commit-pinned references; master is mutable and files are deleted on toolchain bumps by policy
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: If linked at all, a formalizations[] entry with status:'ephemeral' and a commit-SHA-pinned GitHub blob URL; treat as a monitoring feed (what are AI provers producing?) rather than a durable join target
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: lake build at the pinned commit (CI already enforces no sorry/axiom); nightly existence check of the pinned path; auto-expire the link when the file disappears from master — never link master-relative URLs

linkage_opportunities.join_key: Wikidata QID of physics concepts ↔ Physlib decl names (unprefixed, e.g. ACCSystem)
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Same formalizations[] shape with lib='physlib', url=https://physlib.io/docs/<Module>.html#<decl>; restrict to the reviewed Physlib core, exclude PhyslibAlpha (explicitly unmaintained-on-breakage)
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Exact-key lookup in physlib.io/docs/declarations/declaration-data.bmp; record the module path AND decl name separately so a module move (URL break) is distinguishable from a rename (identity break); re-verify nightly — this library has renamed twice (HEPLean→PhysLean→Physlib; physlean.com now 301s to physlib.io), so store decl identity, not URLs, as primary

linkage_opportunities.join_key: fully-qualified decl name + module (disambiguates library); LeanExplore int ids only within one dataset release
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Use hosted API v2 (or the downloadable local dataset, no key) to (a) federate WikiLean's /decl/:name resolver across all 9 indexed libraries and (b) harvest per-decl 'dependencies' arrays — the raw material for the all-of-mathematics dependency graph north star
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Treat LeanExplore as an index, not ground truth: any hit must be re-verified by exact-name lookup in that library's own declaration-data.bmp at the current release (LeanExplore's snapshot lags and still says 'PhysLean'); never persist LeanExplore int ids, persist (library, name)

linkage_opportunities.join_key: 4-char Stacks/Kerodon tag ↔ mathlib decl name, extracted from @[stacks]/@[kerodon] source attributes (523 stacks occurrences / 235 files)
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Grep-extract (attribute syntax is rigid: 4 digits/uppercase, parser-enforced) or run #stacks_tags in a Lean env; add tag-edges to WikiLean's concept graph, giving QID↔decl↔Stacks-tag triangles for algebraic-geometry articles
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Tag format validated by mathlib's own parser at compile time; existence check = HTTP 200 at stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/<TAG> plus title/statement-topic sanity check; the decl side is verified by mathlib CI having compiled the attribute

linkage_opportunities.join_key: decl name (both lists give decl per entry) → QID via WikiLean's existing decl→QID catalog (reverse join through 1000.yaml/@[wikidata] mappings)
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Ingest like 1000.yaml but resolve QID transitively: entry.decl → catalog QID; entries whose decl has no catalog QID go to the conflicts/review queue
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: decl exact-match against mathlib declaration-data; QID assignment inherits the catalog's human-reviewed provenance; conflicts (two entries → one QID, or yaml decl ≠ catalog decl) land in the same review queue pattern as ingest_mathlib_yaml.py's mathlib_yaml_conflicts.jsonl

wikilean_hooks: Schema v4 formalizations[] trigger check: ROADMAP says defer until 'CSLib covers a typical undergrad algorithms course' — measured against today's data, CSLib's Algorithms area is its SMALLEST (56 of 3,210 decls; mergesort + a TimeM time-complexity monad); Computability (578 decls, Cslib.Automata.DA/NA) is the mature area, so the trigger metric may want rewording to Computability coverage.

The @[wikidata] attribute WikiLean's bot pipelines into mathlib is defined in Mathlib/Tactic/CrossRefAttribute.lean and is importable from CSLib right now (CSLib requires mathlib) — a future CSLib tagging bot is the same pipeline pointed at github.com/leanprover/cslib, with the same #wikidata_tags extraction command available.

The /decl/:name resolver (commit ee532c8) can go multi-library by fetching each library's declarations/declaration-data.bmp (all CORS-open, all the same doc-gen4 JSON shape) — one nightly-cached KV blob per library gives O(1) existence checks for mathlib, cslib, physlib, lean-pool.

1000.yaml's own TODO ('generate this file automatically' from a wikidata attribute) means WikiLean's @[wikidata] tags may eventually BE the source of the QID-keyed list catalog/ingest_mathlib_yaml.py consumes — closing the loop, but also meaning the bot's tag quality directly feeds the community site; worth flagging in the property-proposal narrative.

catalog/ingest_mathlib_yaml.py deliberately skips 100.yaml/undergrad.yaml as non-QID-keyed — the transitive decl→QID join through the existing catalog would recover ~100 + ~500 additional mappings with the same conflicts-queue safety pattern already built.

lean-pool's REVIEW_RULES.md is a working, published LLM-review rubric (JSON verdicts, calibration examples, anti-slop findings taxonomy) directly analogous to WikiLean's AI-moderation mission — worth studying for the propose-then-approve pipeline; its daily discovery.yml (Reservoir scan → LLM triage → candidates/discovered.yml) is a template for WikiLean discovering new formalization sources.

LeanExplore's per-decl 'dependencies' arrays across 9 libraries are the cheapest available raw material for the all-of-mathematics dependency graph north star; the local dataset needs no API key, and the autoform:lean-explore MCP skill is already installed in this environment.
reports.quality_mechanism: This cluster is a natural experiment in anti-slop tiers, each explicit about its bar: Mathlib = human maintainer review + CI linter battery (highest). CSLib = mathlib's model transplanted: named maintainers (GOVERNANCE.md), mathlib style guide + mathlibStandardSet linters enforced in lakefile.toml, CslibTests test driver, and mathlib's AI-disclosure policy ("explain which tool(s) you used"). Physlib = curated review for core + PhyslibAlpha lower tier (linters + 'one-look' review, explicitly "we cannot promise to maintain contributions when they break"). lean-pool = NO human content review by design: deterministic gates (builds warning-free, mathlib linters, style checker, no sorry/admit, axiom whitelist = Classical.choice/propext/Quot.sound only, no unsafe/partial, file headers, size limits) + an LLM reviewer with a published rubric (.github/REVIEW_RULES.md: rejects "agent slop: walls of repeated `have`s, dead branches", demands paper-anchored named theorems) + content-pr-guard.yml forbidding anyone (especially AI agents) from touching the checks. merely-true = CI-only (lake build, no errors/sorry/axiom), identity-gated contributor accounts ("anonymous / pseudonymous accounts will not be" approved), auto-merge, and bit-rot handled by deletion. For WikiLean the operational rule this implies: a formalizations[] record's trust level should carry the SOURCE library's tier, and only the declaration-data.bmp existence check is uniform across all tiers.
key_facts: declaration-data.bmp is the universal machine-readable join surface: every doc-gen4 site (mathlib4_docs, api.cslib.io/docs, physlib.io/docs, vilin97.github.io/lean-pool) serves declarations/declaration-data.bmp — JSON mislabeled as image/bmp, CORS `*` — mapping every decl name to {docLink, kind}; it includes all transitive deps, so filter by docLink prefix (./Cslib/ → 3,210; ./Physlib/ → 9,337; ./LeanPool/ → 52,300).

Bare Lean decl names do NOT identify a library: CSLib puts 292 decls into Mathlib namespaces (Language.*, Finset.*), Physlib decls are unprefixed (ACCSystem), lean-pool keeps original project namespaces (bare `ABC`). The join key must be (library, name), and doc-gen4's /find endpoint is client-side JS (returns 200 for garbage) — server-side verification must use declaration-data, not URL status.

Concept duplication across libraries is already live: Mathlib `DFA` vs CSLib `Cslib.Automata.DA` formalize the same concept — schema v4 formalizations[] will face one-QID-to-many-libraries immediately, not hypothetically.

CSLib lives under the leanprover org (NOT leanprover-community), is a Renaissance Philanthropy program with Amazon/Google DeepMind/Stanford CAR sponsorship, whitepaper arXiv:2602.04846 ('CSLib aims to be for computer science what Lean's Mathlib is for mathematics'), and pins mathlib at a commit rev — its docs URL already churned once (leanprover.github.io/cslib/docs 301→ api.cslib.io/docs).

merely-true's maintenance policy is deletion: at each Lean/Mathlib toolchain bump, files that no longer compile are deleted from master (agents invited to restore them); it has zero git tags today, so there is currently NO stable ref except commit SHAs. Contributors so far: Kim Morrison, Vasily Ilin, Bolton Bailey, Kevin Lacker.

lean-pool (117 projects, 841,688 LOC, 52,300 decls, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20513444) has the richest metadata in the Lean world: every project carries MSC2020 codes and named main_declarations with informal statements; 40/117 carry arXiv ids — it is simultaneously a formalization index AND an arXiv/MSC crosswalk.

Mathlib's cross-ref infra (Mathlib/Tactic/CrossRefAttribute.lean) makes @[stacks]/@[kerodon]/@[wikidata] database-agnostic: adding a new database (e.g. an LMFDB or WikiLean-native tag) means one parser + one syntax decl against the shared Database/Tag/tagExt core — a concrete upstreaming path for new cross-references. Example in source: `@[stacks 0052 "(1)"]` on instance NoetherianSpace.set (Topology/NoetherianSpace.lean:75).

Checkout 0d3e25fd (2026-06-27) counts: 523 @[stacks] occurrences across 235 files vs 84 @[wikidata] usages — the Stacks precedent is ~6x ahead; and stacks.math.columbia.edu does NOT link back (verified on tag 0052), so reverse-direction linking is an open niche WikiLean already occupies for Wikipedia.

Physlib renamed twice in two years (HEPLean → PhysLean → leanprover-community/physlib; physlean.com 301s to physlib.io; README badge to its own Stats page is currently a 404) — library identity itself is unstable, so WikiLean's libraries registry needs rename/alias handling at the LIBRARY level, not just the decl level.

LeanExplore (arXiv:2506.11085, by lean-pool co-creator Justin Asher) indexes 9 libraries and ships per-decl AI 'informalization' strings AND decl-level dependency arrays — but its int ids are DB primary keys and its library list still says 'PhysLean', so treat it as a lagging index requiring re-verification, never as ground truth.

Only 1000.yaml among mathlib's curated lists is QID-keyed; 100.yaml uses Freek numbers and undergrad.yaml uses French-agrégation topic strings — and 1000.yaml's header TODO proposes generating it automatically from a wikidata attribute, which would make WikiLean's @[wikidata] pipeline the upstream source of the community 1000.html page.
open_questions: LeanExplore: which commit/release of each indexed library the current dataset pins, and whether ids are stable across dataset releases — needs an API key (free but account-gated; leanexplore.com serves 403 to plain fetch) or a local `lean-explore data fetch` run to inspect.

Mathlib master @[wikidata] count vs WikiLean's records: local checkout (2026-06-27) shows 84 attribute usages, but project memory says 126 merged tags — needs a fresh mathlib pull (or bot/state reconciliation) to know exactly how many tags are live on master today.

Whether Mathlib.Computability (DFA/NFA/TuringMachine) will migrate to or be deprecated in favor of CSLib's Cslib.Automata — the two coexist today; a migration would move ~578 decls' worth of concept mappings across libraries. (Zulip discussion likely; not verified here.)

reports.source: TheoremGraph / TheoremSearch (UW Math AI Lab) — arXiv:2606.25363, theoremsearch.com, api.theoremsearch.com, huggingface.co/uw-math-ai
reports.what_it_is: TheoremGraph (arXiv:2606.25363, "TheoremGraph: Bridging Formal and Informal Mathematics", submitted 2026-06-24, 31pp) is a unified statement-level dependency graph over informal math (11.7M theorem-like environments parsed from arXiv LaTeX, 18.3M directed dependency edges) and formal math (LeanGraph: 388,105 Lean 4 declarations, 11.3M typed edges across 25 Lean projects incl. Mathlib v4.27–v4.29, formal-conjectures, and CSLib), bridged by 47,952 LLM-judged formal↔informal matches. Built by the University of Washington Math AI Lab (students Kurgan/Wang/Leonen/Szeto/Alexander/Remizov + math faculty Jarod Alper, Giovanni Inchiostro, Vasily Ilin; contact vilin@uw.edu). Very alive: the live product theoremsearch.com (semantic search over 9M+ theorems from arXiv, Stacks Project, ProofWiki + 5 more; companion paper arXiv:2602.05216) has a working no-auth REST API + MCP server that I verified live during this research; the flagship HF dataset was last updated 2026-06-28, three days before today. It is an academic-lab product, though — 18 GitHub stars, no SLA, hosting longevity unknown.
reports.data_model: Entities and canonical IDs (verified live via HF datasets-server first-rows and api.theoremsearch.com): (1) STATEMENTS — UUID `statement_id` (e.g. `eedc7b2a-b813-4cd0-9588-29912ab832f7` = Mathlib's `jacobiSym.quadratic_reciprocity'`). Formal statements (table `statement_formal`) carry the load-bearing join fields: `decl_name` (fully-qualified Lean name, e.g. `Lean.instInhabitedStructureInfo`), `module` (e.g. `Lean.Structure`), `file_path`, `kind`, `is_instance`, `docstring`. Informal statements (table `statement_informal`) carry `kind` (theorem/lemma/proposition), `ref` (in-paper number "4.5"), `label` (LaTeX \label key), `lean` (author-written leanblueprint \lean{} decl-name annotation — 1,595 gold pairs), `pre_context`/`post_context`. (2) PAPERS — UUID `paper_id` + `external_id` = arXiv ID (e.g. `2304.01775`) or Lean-repo slug (e.g. `Mathlib_v427`); tables paper_arxiv / paper_lean_community / paper_lean_repo. (3) EDGES — `formal_dependency`: src_id, dep_id, `edge_type` ∈ {sig, proof, def, extends, field, docref} plus position ("conclusion"), binder ("explicit"), role ("fn"), via_proj; `informal_dependency`: src_id, dep_id, cite_key (BibTeX), dep_name ("Lemma 11.27"), location (body/proof), and crucially `methods` = extractor provenance ['deterministic'|'heuristic'|'notation'] — the per-edge precision label. (4) MATCHES (dataset theorem-matching, ~101K rows): query_sid/cand_sid UUIDs, `formal_decl` (e.g. `Prod.eq_iff_fst_eq_snd_eq`), `formal_module` (`Mathlib.Data.Prod.Basic`), formal/informal slogans + bodies, `arxiv_id`, `sim` (cosine), `gpt54_label` ∈ {exact, inexact, wrong}, `deepseek_label`, `source_license`, `license_open` (bool). (5) SLOGANS — one-line NL summaries (Qwen3-235B-generated) for every statement, embedded with Qwen3-Embedding-8B into R^4096, one shared index for both corpora.
reports.access: (A) REST API, base https://api.theoremsearch.com, no auth, no documented rate limits — all verified working 2026-07-01: POST /search (semantic theorem search: query, n_results, sources, authors, types, tags, year_range, citation_weight); GET /graph/embedding?query=...&formality=informal|formal|both (cross-modal search — returned arXiv statements AND a Mathlib node for "quadratic reciprocity" in one call); GET /graph/statement/{uuid}?direction=src|dep|both&formality=formal|informal (returns root {name=decl_name, paper} + dependency nodes with slogans; NOTE: formality=both is REJECTED here despite docs); GET /graph/paper?external_id=2304.01775 (returns paper + 32 statements + 45 edges with methods labels; the /graph/paper/{uuid} path form 404s on bad UUIDs); GET /paper-search, GET /paper-links (full citation edge list). (B) MCP server at https://api.theoremsearch.com/mcp exposing tool `theorem_search` (initialize / tools/list / tools/call). (C) Bulk dumps on HuggingFace (org uw-math-ai): `math-graph` (THE graph release, license cc-by-4.0, 10M–100M rows, 8 CSV configs: paper_arxiv, paper_lean_community, paper_lean_repo, statement_informal, statement_formal, informal_dependency, formal_dependency, slogan; updated 2026-06-28); `theorem-matching` (judged matches, HF tag cc-by-sa-4.0 though the paper says CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 — discrepancy; ~101K rows); `theorem-search-dataset` (9M statements, cc-by-sa-4.0) + `theorem-search-dataset-permissive`. LICENSE CAVEAT: arXiv default license grants no redistribution right, so only 23,399 of 100,799 judged candidates carry open redistributable statement bodies; each record has a `source_license` field, and statement bodies appear nulled in the dump for non-open sources (row0 bodies were null) while the live API serves them. (D) Code: github.com/uw-math-ai/TheoremSearch — 835 commits, pushed 2026-06-21, but NO LICENSE file (gh api reports license: null), so code reuse terms are legally unset.
reports.existing_crosslinks: (1) arXiv — native spine: every informal node carries an arXiv ID (e.g. statement "Lemma 14" of arXiv:2304.01775 is the quadratic-reciprocity formulation I pulled live); references resolved via Semantic Scholar. (2) Mathlib — native: 351,397 declarations (v4.27–v4.29 snapshots) keyed by fully-qualified decl_name, e.g. `jacobiSym.quadratic_reciprocity'`, `Prod.eq_iff_fst_eq_snd_eq` (Mathlib.Data.Prod.Basic), `legendreSym.at_neg_two`. (3) 24 non-Mathlib Lean repos with per-library node/edge counts (Appendix C), including two of WikiLean's mission targets: **formal-conjectures (639 decls)** and **cslib (2,273 decls)**, plus FLT (2,368), PrimeNumberTheoremAnd (5,108), physlib (8,205), carleson, sphere-eversion, pfr, ClassFieldTheory, toric, flt-regular, etc. (4) leanblueprint \lean{} author annotations — 1,595 human-written (informal, formal) pairs across 1,308 informal statements / 1,577 decls, used as gold standard. (5) TheoremSearch's corpus adds ProofWiki and Stacks Project statements (8 sources total). (6) Benchmarked against LeanSearch v2 (MathlibQR fair-810). (7) NO Wikipedia, Wikidata, or MathWorld links anywhere in the paper or data — concept-level linking is exactly the hole WikiLean fills; conversely their arXiv-literature layer is exactly what WikiLean lacks.

linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Exact string join from WikiLean's QID→decl mappings into math-graph statement_formal, giving each WikiLean concept a set of TheoremGraph formal-node UUIDs, then their typed dependency edges and judged arXiv matches

linkage_opportunities.mechanism: QID → decl (WikiLean) → gpt54_label='exact' match rows → arXiv paper + verbatim theorem statement; surface as 'this concept's Mathlib theorem appears as Thm X of arXiv:NNNN' annotations

linkage_opportunities.mechanism: When statement A (matched to a decl under QID₁) depends on statement B (matched to a decl under QID₂) via an informal edge, emit a candidate QID₁→QID₂ 'literature dependency' edge — a third edge class beside WikiLean's Mathlib-dep and Wikidata semantic edges
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Filter to methods=['deterministic'] edges only (98.8% judge-verified precision vs 76.6% heuristic / 42.7% notation — Table 2); require both endpoint matches to be gpt54_label='exact'; aggregate (require ≥2 independent paper witnesses per QID-pair) before proposing; human approval gate

linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Cross-check each decl_name against the source repo at the pinned commit (gh api / doc-gen4 output); edge_type taxonomy (sig/proof/def/extends/field/docref) is kernel-derived from Lean's Environment API, so existence is machine-checked by construction — only the pin/version must be verified

linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Register api.theoremsearch.com/mcp as a tool for the review pipeline: when an annotation claims a theorem is standard/known, the bot retrieves literature witnesses with similarity scores and arXiv links
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Treat results as retrieval evidence, not truth: require the returned slogan/body to be verified against the annotation's claim by the (single, existing) LLM triage step, and record similarity + arxiv_id in provenance so humans can audit; their own eval shows embedding Hit@1 is only 43.5% on gold blueprint pairs — never trust rank-1 blindly

wikilean_hooks: Direct decl_name join TODAY: WikiLean's catalog/data/*.jsonl QID↔decl mappings join math-graph's statement_formal.decl_name with zero new identifier work — same key as the live GET /decl/:name resolver (verified 302 for legendreSym.quadratic_reciprocity), giving each of WikiLean's ~389 concept pages literature-witness candidates from theorem-matching (e.g. legendreSym.at_neg_two ↔ arXiv:2309.02512, sim 0.91, judged exact)

Concept-graph third edge layer: catalog/data/concept_graph.json currently has Mathlib decl-dependencies + Wikidata semantic edges; deterministic-extractor informal edges (98.8% precision) lifted decl→QID add the missing LITERATURE dependency layer for /graph — feed via the nightly ops pipeline as proposals, not writes

The propose-then-approve backend (already LIVE, inert) is the exact anti-slop gate these LLM-judged matches need: ingest gpt54_label='exact' ∧ sim≥0.9 rows as AI proposals on the matching articles, Jack approves — mirrors their own expert-calibration finding that even the strict judge disagrees with an expert 2/10 times

@[wikidata] tagging-bot evidence: for a candidate decl→QID tag, a judged-exact arXiv statement naming the classical theorem is independent textual evidence the tightest-QID agent can cite; conversely WikiLean's human-approved QID↔decl pairs are a concept-level annotation layer TheoremGraph lacks entirely (they have zero Wikidata links) — a contribution to offer upstream (vilin@uw.edu)
Blueprint gold pairs (statement_informal.lean column, 1,595 human \lean{} links) double as a free QA benchmark for WikiLean's own annotation-matching quality
formal-conjectures + CSLib LeanGraph extractions (639/2,273 decls with typed edges) shortcut WikiLean's planned expansion to those two libraries — consume their nodes rather than re-extracting
MCP endpoint api.theoremsearch.com/mcp plugs into the moderation bot stack as a literature-verification tool (fits the one-LLM-triage-step invariant since the tool itself is deterministic retrieval)
reports.quality_mechanism: Layered, provenance-first: (1) Every informal edge carries its extractor label (methods field) so consumers set their own precision floor — deterministic \\ref/\\cite resolution 98.8% judge-verified precision, heuristic prose-cues 76.6%, LLM-notation 42.7% (validated on 500 sampled papers by an independent LLM judge, Kimi K2.5; overall F1 0.642). (2) Formal edges are kernel-derived (Lean 4 elaborator Environment API, post-elaboration, typed 6-way taxonomy; nodes filtered by a shouldIncludeConstant predicate aligned with doc-gen4) — machine-checked by construction. (3) Cross-modal matches use an LLM judge of record (GPT-5.4) chosen AFTER a domain expert found Claude Opus 4.8 over-generous (over-called 'exact' on 5/10 sampled matches); GPT-5.4 was calibrated against the expert (8/10 strict, 9/10 match/non-match) and is rerun-stable (93.2%, κ=0.86); per-cosine-bin verdicts are published (96.2% match in the 0.95–1.0 bin down to 37.4% at 0.80–0.85), plus a lenient second judge (DeepSeek-V4-Pro) for recall comparison. (4) Human gold standard = 1,595 author-written leanblueprint pairs. (5) Per-record source_license provenance. HONEST GAPS the paper itself flags: expert calibration is only 10 pairs ('not every judge-affirmed match is ground truth'), sloganization is lossy, and cross-paper theorem deduplication is explicitly not attempted — so downstream anti-slop still requires WikiLean's human gate.
key_facts: Granularity: TheoremGraph nodes are per-paper printed-statement INSTANCES, not deduplicated theorems — cross-paper restatement matching (i↔i) is explicitly 'not pursued' (Appendix B.4). So the mismatch ladder is: Wikidata QID (1/concept) > Mathlib decl (1/formal statement) ≈ TheoremGraph informal node (1/printed statement/paper, so one theorem = many nodes). WikiLean's QID layer is the only dedup key in the stack; lifting their statement instances to QIDs is a contribution, not a lookup.

Coverage is thin where it matters: only 47,952 of 385,657 swept formal decls (12.4%) got a judged match, and 172,311 (44.7%) had NO informal neighbor at ann_k=50 — most of Mathlib has no literature counterpart in their graph.
43% of all informal edges (7.88M notation-extractor edges) are only 42.7% precise — any consumer MUST filter by the methods label or inherit noise; deterministic-only (5.23M edges) is the safe slice.

Licensing is a three-way tangle: math-graph dump = CC-BY-4.0, theorem-matching HF tag = cc-by-sa-4.0 while the paper text says CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 'gated for non-commercial research', and the GitHub code repo has NO license file at all; arXiv-license restrictions null out statement bodies for all but 23,399 of 100,799 judged candidates in the redistributable release (per-record source_license + license_open fields), though the live API serves full bodies.
A domain expert rejected Claude Opus 4.8 as match judge for being over-generous (5/10 over-calls); GPT-5.4 is judge of record — relevant precedent for WikiLean's own LLM-moderation calibration.

LeanGraph already covers formal-conjectures (639 decls) and CSLib (2,273 decls) — two named targets of WikiLean's join-fabric mission — plus FLT, PrimeNumberTheoremAnd, physlib, carleson, sphere-eversion, pfr, toric and 15 more (Table 18: 388,105 decls, 9,585,510 within-library edges; Mathlib = 90.5% of decls).

Formal and informal graphs have wildly different density (29.2 vs 1.56 edges/statement), and formal slogan similarity decays to random baseline by 3 graph hops while informal stays elevated to 6 — naive cross-graph traversal heuristics will not transfer between the two sides.

Their own premise-selection experiment found graph expansion beats pure embedding search (recall 7.9%→13.4%), and concept retrieval vs chained-premise retrieval need different signals (their QR-tuned config HURT MathlibMPR) — evidence that WikiLean's dependency-graph north star needs graph structure, not just embeddings.
Mathlib snapshot is pinned at v4.27–v4.29 (aggregated), so decl renames since then will silently break joins — WikiLean's /decl 404 is a cheap drift detector.

Live API verified 2026-07-01: no auth, no rate limits documented, one schema bug found (GET /graph/statement rejects formality=both despite docs); hosted by an academic lab (18 GitHub stars) — plan for the dump (HF) as the durable artifact and the API as convenience.
open_questions: Which license actually governs the judged matches — the paper's CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 or the HF card's cc-by-sa-4.0 (no NC)? Materially changes whether WikiLean (public site) can republish match content; needs an email to vilin@uw.edu.
The GitHub repo (uw-math-ai/TheoremSearch) has no LICENSE file — extractor/code reuse terms are legally undefined.

Are statement bodies in the math-graph dump systematically nulled for non-open arXiv licenses (row samples had body:null), and is API access to those bodies itself license-compliant? Determines whether WikiLean must store only statement_ids + arXiv pointers rather than text.
API rate limits, auth plans, and hosting longevity are undocumented — is the academic-lab endpoint safe to build a nightly pipeline on, or should WikiLean mirror the HF dump?

Do the 47,952 judged matches include non-Mathlib project decls (formal-conjectures, cslib), or only Mathlib? The matching swept 385,657 declarations (all 25 projects minus ctors) but per-library match counts are not published.
How often will they refresh the Mathlib snapshot past v4.27–v4.29, and will statement UUIDs be stable across releases (no stated identifier-stability policy)?
Cross-project twin candidates (446 pairs at cosine≥0.85, e.g. NNReal.coe_comp_dconv↔coe_comp_cdconv across add-combi/apap) are cosine-surfaced but never judge-confirmed — usable only as candidates.

Is there interest upstream in QID-level annotation (they have zero Wikidata links)? A WikiLean-contributed decl→QID column would make their graph concept-addressable and is the natural collaboration opener.

reports.source: Semantic search over formal math: LeanExplore (leanexplore.com), LeanSearch v1/v2 (leansearch.net), Loogle (loogle.lean-lang.org), Moogle (moogle.ai); plus LeanFinder and Numina leandex as secondary engines already wired into WikiLean's CLI
reports.what_it_is: Two production semantic search engines over Lean 4 declarations plus one syntactic one. LeanExplore (Justin Asher, independent; paper arXiv:2506.11085) indexes NINE libraries — Batteries, CSLib, FLT, FormalConjectures, Init, Lean, Mathlib, PhysLean, Std — with nightly data builds (latest toolchain 20260620_151737, "Nightly build - 2026-06-20"); repo justincasher/lean-explore is Apache-2.0, actively maintained (last push 2026-06-20). LeanSearch (FrenzyMath / Bin Dong's Peking group; v1 paper arXiv:2403.13310 EMNLP-2024, v2 paper arXiv:2605.13137, May 2026 — note: the tasking's "LeanExplore paper" arXiv id actually belongs to LeanSearch v2) is Mathlib-only but heavily used (11.6M total searches, ~1.08M/week per its live /stats endpoint) and fully open: code (frenzymath/LeanSearch-v2, Apache-2.0) plus bulk corpus AND embedding dumps on HuggingFace. Loogle (nomeata, Apache-2.0, active) is the zero-hallucination syntactic/type-pattern complement; Moogle (Morph Labs, © 2024) is alive but minimal, no public API, and placed last in every published eval.
reports.data_model: Canonical identifier everywhere: the fully-qualified Lean declaration name + module path — exactly WikiLean's existing /decl/:name key. LeanExplore record (SearchResult, Pydantic/openapi.yaml): {id (per-build SQLite primary key, e.g. "238360" — returned as a STRING live despite openapi saying int64), name ("orderOf"), module ("Mathlib.GroupTheory.OrderOfElement"), docstring, source_text (full Lean source), source_link (GitHub URL pinned to a COMMIT SHA, e.g. .../mathlib4/blob/843d7890.../OrderOfElement.lean#L175-L181), dependencies (JSON array of decl names, e.g. ["Nat"]), informalization (LLM-generated, starts "**Bold Title.** ...")}. The paper's underlying unit is the "StatementGroup" (one contiguous source block unifying elaborated sub-declarations) with PageRank score over the dependency graph. LeanSearch record (live POST /search): {name (list of parts), module_name (list), kind, signature, type, value (proof term!), docstring, informal_name ("Order of Element Divides Group Order"), informal_description (LaTeX prose)} + distance (cosine, lower=closer). LSv2 corpus JSONL: declaration kind, FQ name, type signature, value, source location, dependencies, dep-aware bottom-up informalization (Qwen3-32B, Gemini 2.5 Pro fallback), extracted by jixia at pinned Mathlib v4.28.0-rc1. Loogle hit: {name, type, module, doc}. CAUTION: LeanExplore ids are per-nightly-build primary keys — never store them as foreign keys; store name+module+source_link commit.
reports.access: LeanExplore API v2 (key-gated, free key from leanexplore.com after sign-in; Jack's key in ~/.zshrc, live-verified 2026-07-01, 183ms): GET https://www.leanexplore.com/api/v2/search?q=...&limit=20[&packages=Mathlib,FormalConjectures] and GET /api/v2/declarations/{id}, Bearer auth; 429 defined in openapi.yaml but NO rate-limit numbers published anywhere. Cloudflare 403s non-browser User-Agents (Error 1010) before key check — send a browser UA. Python: pip install lean-explore (base=API; [local]=offline hybrid BM25+FAISS+Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B); MCP server: `lean-explore mcp serve --backend api|local` (8 tools: search, search_summary, get_source_code, get_docstring, get_description, get_source_link, get_dependencies, get_module). BULK: public R2 bucket, NO auth — manifest https://pub-48b75babc4664808b15520033423c765.r2.dev/manifest.json lists all toolchains; latest lean_explore.db (SQLite, 2.14 GB, declarations table with informalization+dependencies for all 9 libraries) + informalization_faiss.index (bulk embeddings) + BM25 indices, sha256 per file. Code Apache-2.0. LeanSearch (keyless, no auth): POST https://leansearch.net/search {"query": ["..."], "num_results": ≤150} (batched queries!); GET /stats; POST /fetch exists (records by [module?, name] or id) but my format guess 500'd; docs at leansearch.net/docs (FastAPI/openapi.json). Public deployment = v2 standard mode with cost-reduced Qwen3-Reranker-4B (paper numbers use 8B). BULK: HF datasets FrenzyMath/lsv2-mathlib-v4.28.0-rc1-jsonl (332 MB, one record/decl with informalizations, Apache-2.0) and ...-cuvs (pre-embedded Qwen3-Embedding-8B corpus, Apache-2.0). Loogle: GET https://loogle.lean-lang.org/json?q=... (keyless; &lucky=true 302s to docs anchor), Apache-2.0. Moogle: web only, no API. Mathlib declaration-data ground truth: https://leanprover-community.github.io/mathlib4_docs/declarations/declaration-data.bmp (~65MB JSON).
reports.existing_crosslinks: These engines carry NO Wikidata QIDs and no Wikipedia links — WikiLean would be the first QID join layer over them. What they DO already link: (1) every LeanExplore source_link is pinned to an exact GitHub commit (verified live: mathlib4@843d7890..., google-deepmind/formal-conjectures@4fac2db2...) — machine-checkable provenance; (2) LeanExplore `dependencies` gives a decl-name-keyed dependency edge list across all 9 libraries — a ready-made slice of the "all of mathematics as one graph" north star; (3) FormalConjectures modules encode their SOURCE in the module path — live hit `TwinPrimes.twin_primes` in module `FormalConjectures.Wikipedia.TwinPrimes` (a Wikipedia-article-titled namespace!), others under `FormalConjectures.Paper.*` (arXiv-sourced); LeanExplore is the only semantic index over this; (4) HF dataset cards link arXiv:2605.13137; papers cross-cite Herald (arXiv:2410.10878) and each other; (5) informal_name/informal_description fields are themselves NL↔formal crosslinks (LLM-generated, unverified).
