wikilean_hooks: mathlib_search.py (.claude/skills/mathlib-search/) ALREADY wraps 4 semantic engines (leansearch keyless default, leanfinder, numina SSE, leanexplore key-gated at api/v2/search) + loogle + decl; sem_leanexplore's defensive field-guessing can now be tightened to the verified schema {id,name,module,docstring,source_text,source_link,dependencies,informalization} and gain a packages= param — the single highest-leverage upgrade for cross-library (CSLib/FLT/FormalConjectures/PhysLean) matching

site/search_tools.py exposes these CLIs as fixed-argv prompt-injection-hardened SDK tools to Agent 2, and bot/triage.py's prompt already mandates semantic→decl-verify; a two-engine-agreement rule (LeanSearch + LeanExplore) drops in with no architecture change

Bulk join fabric: nightly lean_explore.db (public R2, no key, sha256-manifested) is a local 9-library table of name→informalization+dependencies+commit-pinned source_link — lets the nightly ops job (site/ops/) refresh decl-level edges for the concept graph offline, respecting the 'no LLM on the git path' invariant since edges are elaborator-derived

/decl/:name resolver is the natural verification endpoint for any candidate produced by these engines; conversely LeanExplore ids must NOT be persisted (per-build keys) — persist name+module+commit SHA, matching the revid-pinning philosophy

FormalConjectures.Wikipedia.* namespace ↔ WikiLean slug is a near-free new annotation class: 'this conjecture has a formal statement (not proof) in FormalConjectures', searchable only through LeanExplore today
Jack already has a working LEANEXPLORE_API_KEY in ~/.zshrc (live-verified) and a /lean-explore skill in the autoform plugin with an MCP server option — no new accounts needed for the API path
key_facts: The tasking conflated two papers: arXiv:2506.11085 is LeanExplore (Asher, June 2025); arXiv:2605.13137 is 'LeanSearch v2: Global Premise Retrieval for Lean 4 Theorem Proving' (Gao et al., May 2026) — the newest and most rigorous eval in this space

Search-as-verifier is NOT safe on rank alone: on the 946-query MathlibQR benchmark the best system (LSv2 rerank) gets nDCG@10 0.623 / Recall@10 0.780; LeanFinder 0.533; LeanExplore 0.393; LeanSearch v1's own bench showed R@10 0.913 but P@10 0.196 (top-10 is mostly non-targets). Use engines as candidate generators, verify by decl existence + commit-pinned source inspection

Live A/B reproduced the gap: 'order of a group element divides the order of the group' → LeanSearch rank-1 orderOf_dvd_card (distance 0.217, correct); LeanExplore top-2 = QuaternionGroup.orderOf_a, orderOf (target missing)
LeanExplore is the ONLY semantic index over CSLib, FLT, FormalConjectures and PhysLean; LeanSearch indexes Mathlib only

Both projects publish bulk dumps: LeanExplore nightly SQLite (2.14GB) + FAISS embeddings on a public no-auth R2 bucket with sha256 manifest; LeanSearch v2 corpus (332MB JSONL) + Qwen3-Embedding-8B cuVS index on HuggingFace, all Apache-2.0

All informalizations are LLM-generated and unverified: LeanExplore paper used Gemini 2.0 Flash (current pipeline default google/gemini-3-flash-preview via OpenRouter); LSv2 uses Qwen3-32B with Gemini 2.5 Pro fallback, dependency-ordered (bottom-up topological) prompting per Herald's finding — grounded, but still slop-capable; formal side (names, types, deps, source) is elaborator-derived and trustworthy
LeanSearch serves ~1.08M searches/week (live /stats), 11.6M all-time — it is infrastructure for the ATP community; public deployment runs a cheaper Qwen3-Reranker-4B than the paper's 8B

LeanExplore evals are LLM-judged RELATIVE rankings only (1st place 55.4±0.7% vs LeanSearch 46.3% vs Moogle 12.0%, Gemini 2.5 Flash judge, 300 AI-generated queries) — no absolute precision; and its result contradicts LSv2's absolute benchmark, so neither engine's self-reported win should be taken as calibrated
LeanExplore ids are per-nightly-build primary keys returned as strings — unstable across builds; the durable key is name+module+source_link commit SHA
leanexplore.com sits behind Cloudflare that 403s (Error 1010) non-browser User-Agents before checking the API key — a 403 is not a bad key (already worked around in the autoform lean-explore skill)

Loogle (loogle.lean-lang.org/json, Apache-2.0, active June 2026) is the zero-false-positive complement: type-pattern hits are elaborator-checked; Moogle is alive but API-less, unmaintained-looking (© 2024), and last in every eval

LSv2's reasoning mode (sketch→retrieve→filter→judge with Claude Sonnet 4.5) recovers only 46.1% of expert-annotated premise GROUPS @10 — global premise retrieval is far from solved; single-decl matching (WikiLean's case) is the easier, better-supported task
reports.quality_mechanism: Formal layer is machine-verified by construction: both pipelines extract from elaborated Lean, not text — LeanExplore via Lake+doc-gen4 per package (StatementGroups from AST; dependency edges and PageRank from elaborator output), LeanSearch v2 via jixia (pinned submodule v4.28.0-rc1) with an is_internal filter dropping compiler-generated names; so names, modules, types, source_text and dependency edges can't be hallucinated. Provenance is pinned: every LeanExplore source_link carries the exact upstream commit SHA; R2 manifest ships sha256 per artifact; LSv2 corpus is pinned to one Mathlib release. Freshness: LeanExplore rebuilds nightly. Retrieval quality is benchmarked in refereed/arXiv papers with released benchmarks (MathlibQR 946 queries, MathlibMPR 69 theorems, FATE-H redistributed CC BY 4.0) and reproduction scripts. THE GAP (anti-slop caveat): the informalizations — the very field semantic search matches against — are LLM-generated with no human or formal verification loop in either project; correctness of an NL↔decl link must therefore be established downstream (decl existence, source inspection at pinned commit, multi-engine agreement), never inferred from a similarity score.

reports.source: Wikimedia ecosystem as join hub: Wikidata math crossref property family, P14534 Mathlib Declaration ID, Wikifunctions, mix'n'match, WDQS/SPARQL federation, and the Gene Wiki playbook
reports.what_it_is: Wikidata (run by Wikimedia Deutschland, the "linked data project for Wikimedia projects" per the wikiedu training module) is the de-facto identifier broker of the open web: items (QIDs) carry external-identifier statements into every math database that matters, plus sitelinks into Wikipedia and Wikifunctions. It is extremely alive: the math crossref family spans ~15 properties with tens of thousands of uses, community-governed via property proposals, constraint reports, and WikiProject Mathematics. Decisive news for WikiLean: the "Mathlib Declaration ID" property was CREATED as P14534 on 2026-06-17 — proposed 2026-06-09 by user "Mynus grey", built explicitly on Mathlib's @[wikidata] attribute, unanimously supported, and already carrying 4 statements. The proposal phase is over; the game is now seeding and stewarding.
reports.data_model: Wikidata: items = QIDs (Q179208 Cayley's theorem, Q863912 prime ideal, Q140128421 Mathlib itself), properties = P-numbers, statements carry qualifiers + references + ranks; datatype "external-id" (1,500-char cap, rendered via formatter URL P1630 with $1 substitution); sitelinks join items to Wikipedia pages AND Wikifunctions ZObjects (site "wikifunctionswiki"). The math crossref family (main-graph use counts, 2026-07-01, via SPARQL COUNT): P2812 MathWorld ID 6,763 (Q179208→"CayleysGroupTheorem"); P4215 nLab ID 4,329; P1563 MacTutor biography ID 3,078; P6781 ProofWiki ID 2,556; P894 zbMATH Open document ID 1,769; P7554 Encyclopedia of Mathematics article ID 1,212 (Q923480 jet→"Jet"); P7726 PlanetMath ID 937; P829 OEIS ID 444 (Q2345603 plastic ratio→"A060006"); P12888 Metamath statement ID 438 (Q179208→"cayley"); P3285 Mathematics Subject Classification ID 377 (Q1048447 multiplicative function→"11N37"); P12934 The House of Graphs ID 212 (Q3113838 Desargues graph→"1036"); P9526 Scholarpedia article ID 209; P11497 DLMF ID 199 (Q179467 Fourier series→"1.8.E1"); P12987 LMFDB knowl ID 109 (Q828111 zero divisor→"ring.zero_divisor", formatter https://www.lmfdb.org/knowledge/show/$1 — knowls/glossary ONLY, no object-level LMFDB property exists); P14106 Erdős Problem number 3; P14534 Mathlib Declaration ID 4 (Q863912→"Ideal.IsPrime", Q179208→"Equiv.Perm.subgroupOfMulAction", Q192760 FTA→"Complex.isAlgClosed", Q11567→"Complex"); plus P2534 defining formula 113,056 (LaTeX payload, not an ID) and P4839 Wolfram Language entity code 407,372. arXiv: P818 arXiv ID = 351 on main graph but 464,220 on the scholarly split graph; P820 arXiv classification and paper-level joins live there too (concept→paper via P921 main subject). P14534's record: format regex P1793 = [\p{L}_][\p{L}\p{N}_'.!?]*, formatter URL = https://leanprover-community.github.io/mathlib4_docs/find/?pattern=$1#doc (verified HTTP 200; JS redirect off declaration-data), related-property P1659 → P12888/P6781/P4215/P2812, source website P1896 → github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4, type constraint = mathematical object/concept/theorem. Wikifunctions ZObjects: Z8 function (Z8K3 = tester list, Z8K4 = implementation list), e.g. Z21187 sieve of Eratosthenes with testers Z21210/Z21211 — verification = testers, no formal-spec field.
reports.access: SPARQL: https://query.wikidata.org/sparql (anonymous, format=json, User-Agent header required, ~60s timeout) — NOTE the 2025 graph split: scholarly articles are only on https://query-scholarly.wikidata.org/sparql. Entity JSON: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/Q179208.json (works for P-entities too). MediaWiki API: https://www.wikidata.org/w/api.php (wbgetentities, search, revisions). Bulk: weekly JSON entity dumps at dumps.wikimedia.org. Writes: QuickStatements (quickstatements.toolforge.org, needs autoconfirmed account) and mix'n'match (mix-n-match.toolforge.org/api.php?query=catalogs). Wikifunctions API: https://www.wikifunctions.org/w/api.php?action=wikilambda_fetch&zids=Z21187; site stats via meta=siteinfo (28,058 content pages; main page states 4,489 functions; 135 active users). LICENSE: all Wikidata structured data is CC0 1.0 (imports need no permission; WikiLean's mapping dataset must be releasable CC0 to import). Wikipedia text CC BY-SA 4.0. WDQS federation SERVICE allowlist (mediawiki.org/wiki/Wikidata_Query_Service/User_Manual/SPARQL_Federation_endpoints): UniProt, WikiPathways, MeSH, Rhea, SwissLipids, IDSM/ELIXIR, DBpedia, Getty, Europeana, Nobel, EPO — ZERO math endpoints (no math DB runs a public SPARQL endpoint).
reports.existing_crosslinks: One QID already joins informal + formal math: Q179208 Cayley's theorem carries P2812 "CayleysGroupTheorem" (MathWorld) + P12888 "cayley" (Metamath) + P14534 "Equiv.Perm.subgroupOfMulAction" (Mathlib) + Freebase/Quora/Treccani/Microsoft-Academic IDs simultaneously. Measured pairwise join density on the main graph: Metamath∩MathWorld = 321 items, Metamath∩ProofWiki = 174, nLab∩MathWorld = 968, OEIS∩MathWorld = 248. Wikidata→Wikifunctions: 1,923 wikifunctionswiki sitelinks (Q177898 sieve of Eratosthenes → Z21187; Q17987729 22/7 → Z22037; many are language ZObjects like Q1775361 Gondi → Z1658). Mix'n'match community-matching catalogs already live: Metamath #6372 (1,295 entries), MathWorld #3523 (15,347), ProofWiki definitions #2781 (13,110) + axioms #2780 (116), EoM #1502 (8,421), nLab #2261 (13,752) — no Mathlib catalog yet. The leanprover-community 1000+ theorems project (1000-plus.github.io) keys every theorem file by QID (repo _thm/ contains Q1008566.md, Q1032886.md, ...). Metamath and Mathlib are the ONLY formal libraries with properties — no Isabelle/AFP, Coq/Rocq, Mizar, Agda, or HOL property exists (regex sweep over all property labels).
reports.quality_mechanism: Layered community gatekeeping, all observed working on P14534 within days: (1) property proposals require public discussion + consensus before a property exists (P14534: floated on Wikidata talk:WikiProject Mathematics 2026-05-29, 慈居 pinged 40+ WikiProject members, supports from The Anome/慈居/The-erinaceous-one, created by property-creator Yirba 2026-06-17); (2) machine-checked property constraints (format regex, type, scope, entity-type) with auto-generated violation reports — Wikidata:Database_reports/Constraint_violations/P14534 ran 2026-06-19 and caught exactly 1 violation (Q863912 missing a mathematical-concept type); (3) formatter URLs are locked ("cannot be changed without community consensus" per Wikidata:External_identifiers) and stability of derived links is the make-or-break review question (慈居 asked it verbatim; mathlib4_docs/find answered it); (4) mix'n'match human matching for bulk imports; (5) the Gene Wiki playbook for bot-scale correctness (eLife 52614): WikidataIntegrator library + Jenkins-scheduled syncs, per-statement provenance references including source version, "detection and preservation of conflicting human edits", monthly diff reports, and round-tripping community corrections upstream (98.9% of 2,007 disease mappings validated and folded into the next Disease Ontology release). A cautionary precedent: The Anome's follow-up proposal to store raw Lean proof text in items was withdrawn within hours (1,500-char cap; "Wikidata can't check proofs") — Wikidata wants pointers, not payloads.

The WDQS graph split (2025) silently breaks paper queries: P818 arXiv ID shows 351 on query.wikidata.org but 464,220 on query-scholarly.wikidata.org — any literature join MUST target the scholarly endpoint.

Metamath (P12888) and Mathlib (P14534) are the only formal proof libraries with Wikidata properties; no Isabelle/Coq/Mizar/Agda/HOL property exists — Wikidata's formal-math hub role is young and WikiLean is at its center.

A proposal to store raw Lean proof code in Wikidata items was floated by The Anome (2026-06-07) and withdrawn the same day (1,500-char cap; 'Wikidata can't check proofs') — the community wants external identifiers, not payloads, which is exactly the P14534/WikiLean-resolver architecture.
The leanprover-community 1000+ theorems project already uses QIDs as its canonical file-level keys (_thm/Q1008566.md) — the QID-as-spine bet is independently confirmed by the Lean community itself.
LMFDB's only property (P12987, 109 uses) covers knowls (glossary), not mathematical objects — there is no way today to link a QID to elliptic curve 11.a2; that property doesn't exist yet.

The WDQS federation allowlist contains zero math endpoints (all life-science/cultural: UniProt, MeSH, Rhea, Getty...) — the biology world built federated SPARQL, math has not; Wikidata co-location is currently the ONLY live cross-math-database join mechanism.

The Gene Wiki numbers show the ceiling: 1.1M genes + 940k proteins synced via WikidataIntegrator/Jenkins with per-statement versioned references, human-edit preservation, and 98.9% community-validated mappings fed back upstream — the exact playbook (at 1/1000 scale) for @[wikidata]↔P14534 syncing.

Constraint reports work fast: P14534's auto-generated violation report ran 2 days after creation and caught 1 real error (Q863912 lacking a mathematical-concept type) — free QA infrastructure for every ID WikiLean imports.
Local mathlib checkout has ~83 real @[wikidata] tags vs ~126 claimed merged in the docs — the checkout lags; refresh before regenerating property_seed.tsv.

Cross-database join density measured: 968 QIDs carry both nLab and MathWorld IDs, 321 both Metamath and MathWorld — thousands of concepts are already multi-homed, so WikiLean's graph nodes can pick up 3-6 external mirrors each for free.

Wikifunctions is real but small and Lexeme-focused: 4,489 functions, 1,923 QID sitelinks, verification = testers only (Z8K3), no formal-spec field — the spec/verification niche WikiLean's wikifunctions/ sub-project targets is genuinely empty.

reports.source: Adjacent prior art cluster: Formal Abstracts; academic math knowledge graphs (MaRDI, zbMATH Open, OntoMathPRO, ULO, MathGloss); map-of-mathematics visualizations (Quanta, Domain of Science, Paperscape); arXiv-as-database + theorem extraction (arXMLiv, TheoremKB, TheoremSearch/TheoremGraph); Stacks Project / Kerodon tag system; OEIS crosslinking practice
reports.what_it_is: Six bodies of prior art surrounding WikiLean's mission, each investigated at the primary source. Liveness varies sharply: Formal Abstracts (Hales, 2017) is dead (last push to formalabstracts/formalabstracts 2019-11-05; successor CNL-CIC stalled 2021-03); OntoMathPRO is decaying (ontomathpro.org serves a mismatched *.github.io TLS cert); MaRDI, zbMATH Open, OEIS, Stacks, arXiv, and the UW Math AI Lab's TheoremSearch/TheoremGraph (papers Feb/Jun 2026, live API+MCP) are actively maintained. The single most important discovery: TheoremGraph (arXiv:2606.25363, UW Math AI Lab) has already built a draft of the north-star — an 11.7M-node informal theorem graph from arXiv joined to a 388,105-node LeanGraph — but with LLM-judged links at 48-87% precision and no moderation layer, i.e. exactly the slop WikiLean's human+AI moderation is built to fix.
reports.data_model: FORMAL ABSTRACTS: 'fabstract' = Lean-grounded formal statement (not proof) of a paper's main results; never got past design phase; no stable ID scheme ever shipped. MaRDI: Wikibase instance — own Q/P namespace NOT aligned with Wikidata numbering (example item Q3045 = a DLMF formula; example property P4 in queries like `SELECT ?item WHERE { ?item wdt:P4 wd:Q1399 }`); items imported from 8 sources (DLMF formulas, swMATH software, zbMATH Open + arXiv + Crossref publications, Zenodo, polyDB, CRAN); each imported item keeps 'Wikidata QID'/'Wikidata PID' properties pointing back, plus an internal SQL mapping table. zbMATH Open: document IDs (Zbl/DE numbers), author IDs, MSC 2020 codes; fields include DOI, arXiv ID, references, swMATH software links. ULO (KWARC): RDF upper ontology for formal libraries, namespace http://mathhub.info/ulo, predicates ulo:defines, ulo:contains, ulo:crossrefs, ulo:extends, ulo:imports/dc:requires; Isabelle and Coq exports exist (arXiv:2005.08884). MathGloss: term→QID alignment CSVs (title,link[,suggestion] format) over 5 sources (Chicago glossaries, French curriculum with Lean 4 links, MuLiMa, nLab, Wikidata); QID is the join key; also extracts 'Mathlib extends' graphs into Neo4j. reports.quality_mechanism: Per-source anti-slop mechanisms, strongest to weakest: OEIS — every new sequence and edit passes human editorial review before publication, plus full git history for provenance. Stacks Project — single-editor (de Jong) curation with community comments; tags are append-only and tombstone instead of vanish. zbMATH Open — professional reviewer/editor workflow assigns MSC codes and reviews; author disambiguation is curated. arXiv — moderation (endorsement, category screening) but NO refereeing: metadata is reliable, mathematical content is not; treat arXiv links as bibliographic, not truth-bearing. MaRDI — imports only from vetted databases (DLMF, zbMATH, swMATH...) with Wikibase provenance/references per statement. Mathlib CrossRefAttribute — tags ride the normal Mathlib PR review + CI path (the same human gate WikiLean's bot already respects). TheoremGraph/TheoremSearch — LLM-judge validation only (48-87% precision by cosine tier), openly reported: a candidate generator, not a source of truth. MathGloss — heuristic/LLM alignment with no review loop. Formal Abstracts — Lean typechecking was to be the mechanism; it never materialized, and the project died. The cluster-wide pattern: every source that survived pairs stable, never-reused IDs with a HUMAN editorial gate; every LLM-only pipeline either labels itself candidate-quality or dies — which is precisely WikiLean's propose-then-approve thesis.

reports.what_it_is: An annotated-Wikipedia math mirror where every statement carries its Mathlib formalization status, run solo by Jack McCarthy with heavy AI moderation. Three co-resident subsystems: the live Cloudflare Worker wiki (wiki/, Hono + D1 + KV), the @[wikidata] tagging bot that lands human-reviewed tags in leanprover-community/mathlib4 (bot/, batch 6 / PR #41139 in flight), and a management control plane (manage/) that turns the QID-keyed concept graph into ranked worklists. Very alive: nightly launchd ops at 03:00, GitHub Actions poller, 5 merged mathlib batches, digest refreshed within 19h of this audit.
reports.data_model: THE JOIN SPINE IS THE WIKIDATA QID, present on every layer. (1) Concept graph catalog/data/concept_graph.json: {nodes:[{qid:"Q1000660", label:"Algebra over a field", slug:"Algebra_over_a_field", primary_decl:"Algebra", module:"Mathlib.Algebra.Algebra.Defs", status:"formalized|not_formalized", importance:"High"}], edges:[{from:"Q176623", to:"Q516776", source:"mathlib", decls:[["AEMeasurable","MeasurableSpace"],...]} | {from,to,source:"wikidata", props:[{p:"P527",p_label:"has part(s)"}]}]} — 1,376 nodes / 13,302 edges (12,237 mathlib-evidence edges carrying the underlying decl pairs; 1,065 wikidata semantic edges). NOTE: verified (merged-tag) and coverage/n_formalized/n_status fields are NOT in concept_graph.json — site/build_graph_page.py stamps them at page-build time from bot/data/tagged_in_master.txt and manage/data/coverage.json. Builders: catalog/mathlib_deps/extract_targets.py (concept_layer → targets.txt 2,725 decls + decl_to_qid.json 2,725 entries), extract_deps.lean (Expr.getUsedConstants over a built mathlib → mathlib_edges.tsv 9,999 decl→decl edges), fetch_wikidata_edges.py (WDQS induced subgraph → wikidata_edges.jsonl 1,126 edges), merge_graph.py (lifts decl edges to QID edges via decl_to_qid). (2) Concept layer catalog/data/concept_layer.jsonl (1,376 QIDs; 815 formalized/561 not): {qid, titles[], primary_title, article_slug, class, importance, status, primary_decl, module, confidence, secondary_decls:[{decl,module}], no_match_reason, provenance:"ai", source:"wikilean-tagging"} — the declared single source of truth for QID→Mathlib; 924 QIDs collapse multiple article titles. (3) D1 articles table (760 live articles): slug PK, wikipedia_title, display_title, wikidata_qid, revid, annotations (JSON blob), version, schema_version (=3 everywhere), latest_revid, last_upstream_check, n_formalized/n_partial/n_not_formalized; plus revisions (kind: edit|revert|seed|pipeline|contribution, meta JSON), moderation_state (proposal blob, rejected_proposals), annotation_events (field-level change log), flags, proposals (lifecycle table, migration 0009). (4) Annotation record (wiki/src/validation.ts + corpus): {id:"538b179931e6" (12-hex), kind:"theorem|definition|proposition|example|...", label, anchor:{section, snippet}, status:"formalized|partial|not_formalized|rejected", mathlib:{decl:"ProbabilityTheory.tendstoInDistribution_inv_sqrt_mul_sum_sub", module:"Mathlib.Probability.CentralLimitTheorem", match_kind:"exact|generalization|special_case|invocation|related"}, note, provenance:"human|ai-moderated|ai|ai-agent1"} — 34,366 annotations on disk mirror; per-annotation QID does NOT exist yet (QID is article-level only); schema v4 = multi-library formalizations[] is deferred with a written trigger. (5) Decl side: wiki/public/assets/decl-index/ = 411,273 fully-qualified Mathlib names in 849 prefix shards (doc-gen4 declaration-data), consumed by GET /decl/:name and editor autocomplete. (6) manage/data/: centrality.json (PageRank per QID; rank flows toward foundational concepts because mathlib edge A→B = A depends on B), coverage.json (per-article, joined by SLUG not QID — deliberate: upstream noise double-assigns QIDs, e.g. Q199 and Q310395 both carry slug "1"), worklists, digest.json.
reports.access: Live HTTP: GET https://wikilean.jackmccarthy.org/decl/:name — 302 to mathlib4_docs, or with Accept: application/json returns {ok, decl, module, docs_url, cited_by:[{slug,id,label,status}]} (reverse citations from KV blob declcites:v1, Cache-Control 3600s); /graph + /graph_data.json (KV-first, key graph:data:v1); /stats, /proposals, /recent-changes; GET /api/work (bearer). Wikidata formatter URL committed in the property proposal: https://wikilean.jackmccarthy.org/decl/$1. Files (git, repo Deicyde/WikiLean): catalog/data/*.jsonl (concept_layer, articles 29,135 rows, wikidata_universe 11,681 QIDs, pilot/tier2/generated/mathlib_yaml/refresh tagged layers), concept_graph.json/.graphml, wikilean_mathlib.ttl (Turtle, wl: namespace https://wikilean.org/ns#), bot/data/property_seed.tsv (127 rows) + property_seed_quickstatements.csv (QS v1, PXXXX placeholder) + tagged_in_master.txt (126 QIDs) + corrections.jsonl, site/annotations/*.json (disk is cache/backup; D1 is canonical — never re-seed from disk). Nightly artifacts push via `wrangler kv key put` (no Worker deploy). LICENSE: article text CC BY-SA 4.0 (attribution footer on every page), annotations CC0 (stated in the P0 footer work + CONTRIBUTING.md data notice).

linkage_opportunities.join_key: library-qualified decl name; article slug/QID from a WikiProject Computer Science catalog run
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: The roadmap names this exact trigger: 'Schema v4 formalizations[] + libraries registry — trigger: CSLib covers a typical undergrad algorithms course.' Seams: (1) validation.ts grows a LIBRARIES registry const next to ANNOTATION_STATUSES; (2) annotations[].formalizations[] with library field; (3) catalog/fetch_catalog.py re-run with Template:WikiProject_Computer_science to extend articles.jsonl; (4) Agent 2 multi-checkout (roadmap P3 line item) so tag_with_mathlib.py-style agents grep a CSLib checkout; (5) decl.ts per-library index as above.
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Same anti-hallucination oracle as Mathlib: decl existence against a CSLib doc-gen index at save time (server-side validation seam exists — P0 shipped shared-enum validation in POST /api/article/:slug); provenance stamping + annotation_events log every field change; @[wikidata]-style upstream tags into CSLib would inherit the bot's ≥2-reviewer gate if that pipeline is pointed at a second repo.

linkage_opportunities.join_key: QID (node identity) or decl pair (edge evidence)
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: concept_graph.json edges are already source-discriminated ({source:"mathlib"|"wikidata"} with per-source evidence payloads decls[]/props[]); merge_graph.py is a ~150-line pure-Python merger that takes edge files keyed to QIDs — a third edge source is one more input file + one more source value. manage/centrality.py PageRank consumes whatever edges the graph carries (it only reads from/to/source), so worklist prioritization inherits new edges for free. Nightly ships it via `wrangler kv key put graph:data:v1` — no deploy.
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Follow the mathlib-edge precedent: an edge is only admitted with machine-checkable evidence attached (the decl-pair list from Expr.getUsedConstants, or the Wikidata P-property id) — never bare QID adjacency. External edges must carry their provenance payload and be reproducible by re-running the fetcher; edges whose endpoints aren't in the node set are dropped by both merge_graph.py and centrality.py load_graph (defense in depth already coded).

linkage_opportunities.join_key: arXiv id / DOI, attached at two levels: annotation references[] (schema v4) and decl→paper edges harvested from Mathlib docstring citations
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: (1) The /decl/:name JSON response already returns cited_by[] — extending the declcites:v1 blob builder (site/build_decl_citations.py, deterministic, nightly) to also emit decl→arXiv links harvested from doc-gen docstrings is a pure-artifact change, zero Worker deploy (same KV pattern). (2) Statement level: references:[{kind:"arxiv", id:"2001.12345"}] rides the v4 migration. (3) Concept level: Wikidata P-properties ('described by source', main-subject backlinks) via the existing fetch_wikidata_edges.py WDQS path.
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: arXiv id syntactic validation + existence check against the arXiv API before the nightly artifact is written (the builder already tolerates partial data — 'a partial write must not sink the whole index'); Wikidata-mediated links are verified by the P-statement existing on the QID (SPARQL via wikidata.py, UA already configured); no LLM on this path, keeping the bot pipeline's determinism invariant.

linkage_opportunities.join_key: QID + fully-qualified decl name via the pending 'Mathlib declaration' external-ID property
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: bot/export_property_seed.py already emits property_seed_quickstatements.csv (QS v1 with PXXXX placeholder) from the 126 merged tags; once the property is created, replace the placeholder and submit — after Jack's personal review (standing rule, encoded in memory). The formatter URL https://wikilean.jackmccarthy.org/decl/$1 is live and load-bearing.
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Triple gate already implemented: (1) git-grep of mathlib MASTER for each `wikidata Qxxx` attribute (must equal tagged_in_master.txt); (2) the attribute line's following declaration must name-match the catalog decl or the row goes to property_seed_flags.tsv; (3) batch attribution against the 5 merged PR diffs. Rows failing any check are flagged, not exported.

wikilean_hooks: QID-keyed node set: any external dataset that reconciles to Wikidata QIDs plugs into concept_graph.json as node attributes or evidence-bearing edges via catalog/mathlib_deps/merge_graph.py; PageRank (manage/centrality.py) and the worklists inherit it automatically.

Provenance-layer catalog pattern: bot/pool.py CATALOG = [pilot_tagged, tier2_tagged, generated_candidates (human-verified), mathlib_yaml_tagged (maintainer-reviewed), refresh_tagged] with later-file-wins precedence — a new external mapping source is one more jsonl layer, no schema change.

GET /decl/:name resolver (wiki/src/decl.ts): name→module via 849-shard/411,273-name doc-gen index, 302 to mathlib4_docs or JSON with cited_by[]; the seam for multi-library support is per-library manifest directories + the declcites:v1 KV blob.

Nightly no-deploy artifact channel: build a JSON artifact deterministically, `wrangler kv key put` (graph:data:v1, declcites:v1 precedents in site/ops/nightly-moderate.sh lines 108-128) — new join fabrics ship without touching the Worker.

Schema v4 landing zone: articles.schema_version column exists (=3); v4 = annotations[].formalizations[] (multi-library) MUST bundle with the annotation-ID backfill (one corpus rewrite); render-cache prefix render:v14 must bump with it.

Annotation-level provenance + moderation stack as the correctness substrate for ALL external links: findLostHuman 422 floor, stampProvenance server-side, annotation_events field-level log, flags, propose-then-approve (proposals table + moderation_state.proposal) — external-source corrections to human-touched rows should flow through proposals, not writes.

Agent-2 verification MCP (site/search_tools.py): fixed-argv, read-only, injection-hardened wrappers of mathlib_search.py (loogle/semantic/decl) + wikidata.py (search/entity/xrefs/sitelinks/sparql/reconcile) — add one wrapper per new external source so annotators can verify links in-loop.
tagged_in_master.txt (126 QIDs) is the human-reviewed GROUND-TRUTH tier — build_graph_page.py stamps verified on exactly these nodes; any cross-source evaluation should treat this set as gold labels.
Wikifunctions join (build_join.py, sitelink-based ZID↔QID) is the template for joining any third identifier system through Wikidata sitelinks/external-IDs.
key_facts: Scale right now: 1,376 concept-graph nodes / 13,302 edges (12,237 mathlib + 1,065 wikidata); 760 live D1 articles; 34,366 annotations on disk (33,090 status-bearing: 27.5% formalized / 14.1% partial / 58.4% not); 1,271 extracted statements on 31 articles awaiting Agent-2; 29,135-article catalog; 11,681 tracked QIDs; 2,725 decl→QID targets; 126 merged @[wikidata] tags; 411,273-name decl index; pool of 109 fresh tag candidates (~4.4 batches).

Mathlib graph edges are EVIDENCE-BACKED: each QID→QID edge stores the underlying decl pairs from Expr.getUsedConstants (same reference notion doc-gen4 hyperlinks) — the anti-slop precedent every new edge source must match.

The graph node fields 'verified' and 'coverage' the mission brief assumes are NOT in concept_graph.json — they are stamped at build time by site/build_graph_page.py from tagged_in_master.txt and manage/data/coverage.json; a fabric consuming the raw file must re-join them.
The safe article join key is SLUG, not QID: Wikidata noise double-assigns QIDs (Q199 and Q310395 both map to slug '1'); manage/coverage.py and the worklists dedupe by slug deliberately.

Per-annotation QIDs do not exist — QID lives at article level only; match_kind (exact 7,246 / generalization 2,476 / special_case 1,128 / invocation 765) is the fine-grained statement↔decl semantics, and the physlib roadmap note requires a distinct match_kind for statement-only stubs (directly applicable to formal-conjectures).

Provenance distribution is extremely AI-heavy: 32,263 ai / 1,964 ai-agent1 / 109 ai-moderated / 30 human — the human tier is tiny but is the protected floor (findLostHuman 422, exact string matching on the enum: never rename).

D1 is canonical, disk is mirror, and the graph refreshes on a different cadence — status.py currently reports the graph STALE vs annotations; any fabric build must run merge_graph.py first or consume KV graph:data:v1 knowing its build date.

The Wikidata property proposal stores the decl NAME (Option A) precisely so links survive Mathlib module refactors — the /decl resolver absorbs module churn; external systems should link via /decl/:name, never via module paths.

The corrections feedback loop is machine-readable: bot/data/corrections.jsonl records reviewer verdicts with failure_mode taxonomy (wrong_decl, qid_too_broad) + suggested fixes, harvested from PR reviews and fed back as few-shot — a reusable pattern for cross-source link QA.
No LLM on the git path: the ONLY LLM in the tagging pipeline is triage.py; everything landing in mathlib or Wikidata artifacts is fetch/parse/git/gh — a hard invariant new integrations must respect.
Licensing is clean for a join fabric: annotations CC0, article text CC BY-SA 4.0 with attribution footer; wikilean_mathlib.ttl already models the export as Wikidata-aligned RDF (wl: namespace).

Schema v4 has a contractual migration rule: formalizations[] must land bundled with the annotation-ID backfill in ONE corpus rewrite, with a render-cache prefix bump — external-library integrations are gated behind this single migration.
reports.quality_mechanism: Layered, and unusually explicit: (1) upstream ground truth — @[wikidata] tags only merge into mathlib4 after ≥2 human reviewers incl. ≥1 maintainer plus mathlib CI; the merged set (tagged_in_master.txt) is re-derived by git-grepping master, and export_property_seed.py re-verifies each row's attribute-adjacent declaration against the catalog before Wikidata submission (mismatches → flags file). (2) In-wiki floor — findLostHuman: any bot save that drops/alters a provenance:'human' annotation (tombstones included) 422s; stampProvenance prevents laundering ai→human; optimistic concurrency (version CAS, 409); server-side enum/length/payload validation; decl names checkable against the 411k doc-gen index. (3) Audit trail — annotation_events logs field-level diffs on EVERY write path; revisions carry kind/meta (run_id, model, mathlib_sha); anonymous reader flags with a reason taxonomy feed the /api/work priority queue. (4) Propose-then-approve — AI never mutates human annotations; proposals table records full lifecycle incl. rejections with reason enums, and rejected deltas are never re-proposed (fields_sig dedupe). (5) Feedback learning — harvest_corrections.py/apply_corrections.py turn reviewer verdicts (failure_mode: qid_too_broad, wrong_decl) into few-shot guidance for the tightest-QID triage agent. (6) Determinism — no LLM on the git path; manage/ is pure-Python offline; graph edges carry reproducible evidence payloads. (7) Product invariant — articles.revid advances only atomically with re-anchored annotations (stale-but-consistent guarantee).
open_questions: Does an LMFDB external-ID property on Wikidata cover enough of the 1,376 concept QIDs to make QID the primary LMFDB join, or is object-level (label-based) linking the real value? Needs the LMFDB-side researcher to report coverage.

Per-annotation QIDs (statement-level concept identity) have no designed slot — do they ride the v4 formalizations[] migration or a separate anchors-adjacent field? The one-corpus-rewrite rule says they must be decided BEFORE v4 ships.
The 21,498 mathlib blocks with match_kind null are mostly not_formalized rows, but that should be verified before any cross-source evaluation treats match_kind as complete.

concept_layer.jsonl is 100% provenance 'ai' (human review 'can upgrade in place later' — none has); only the 126 merged-tag QIDs are human-verified — external fabrics must not treat the other ~1,250 mappings as ground truth.

graph:data:v1 and declcites:v1 KV keys are unversioned-by-content — if the node schema grows (lmfdb, stated_in), does the key bump to v2 or is the viewer tolerant? build_graph_page.py's viewer JS reads fields by name, so additive is probably safe, but untested.
The wikifunctions join found only 32/1,904 ZIDs mapped — is the bottleneck WikiLean's 1,376-concept coverage or Wikifunctions' lack of QID sitelinks? Determines whether that seam scales.

reports.source: Wikidata Mix'n'match tool mechanics — primary sources: https://mix-n-match.toolforge.org (live api.php probed 2026-07-02), https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Mix%27n%27match/Manual, https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Mix%27n%27match/Import, https://github.com/magnusmanske/mixnmatch_rs (deployed Rust rewrite; cloned and read: IMPORT_FORMAT.md, src/api/router.rs, src/api/matching.rs, src/api/sync.rs, src/api/data.rs (prep_match_claim), src/import_catalog.rs, src/meta_entry.rs, src/wd_match_sync.rs, src/microsync.rs, html/vue-components/import.js + mnm-mixins.js), plus live Wikidata API for P12888/P14534.
reports.what_it_is: Magnus Manske's Toolforge tool for matching entries of external catalogs to Wikidata items. A catalog = external database bound to (usually) one Wikidata external-ID property; entries = {ext_id, name, description, optional URL/type}; the community matches entries to QIDs via List mode, Match mode, a distributed game, and automatchers. 5,872 catalogs exist (live count). It is the standard community-validation channel for exactly WikiLean's shape of problem: the "Metamath statement" catalog #6372 (P12888, 1,295 entries, created via import by ordinary user "Uniwah" in July 2024, 388 human-matched) is a direct precedent; NO Mathlib/Lean catalog exists yet (verified by scanning all 5,872 catalog names/descriptions).
reports.data_model: Catalog metadata: id, name, desc, url, type (e.g. "science"), search_wp (search-language wiki), wd_prop (property binding, e.g. 12888), wd_qual (qualifier — breaks sync, avoid), owner/username, active flag, source_item (QID for P248 references), autoscrape JSON, do_auto_update. Entry: ext_id (the external ID — for WikiLean this is the Mathlib decl name, the P14534 value), ext_name, ext_desc (≤255 chars), ext_url (auto-generated from the property's formatter URL if omitted), type (Qxxx), q, user, timestamp; plus optional auxiliary Pxxx=value rows, multilingual descriptions/aliases, person dates, coordinates, mnm_relations, kv_entries. Match states: unmatched (q null) → preliminary/automatched (q>0, user=0) → fully matched (q>0, user>0) → N/A (not a Wikidata thing). Reserved user IDs: 0=automatic, 3=date match, 4="Auxiliary data matcher" (Wikidata-derived), 5=location. Import formats: (a) TSV/CSV with one header row, columns id+name mandatory, optional desc/url/type/q/autoq/born/died/Pxxx — CAUTION: in current code (src/meta_entry.rs) BOTH "q" and "autoq" columns set user=4, i.e. fully matched; (b) MetaEntry JSON/JSONL via POST /api/v1/import_catalog with modes add_replace (default; never deletes) and add_replace_delete (deletes absentees except fully-matched, which are NEVER deleted); JSON path enforces user ∈ {0, importing-user} ("can't blame user X" guard) — q + user=0 imports as preliminary, q + own uid as confirmed. Re-import of a fully-matched entry keeps existing q/user/timestamp (human verdicts survive refreshes).
reports.access: UI: https://mix-n-match.toolforge.org/#/catalog/{id}, /#/entry/{id}, /#/import (catalog creation; tests up to 1000 rows then queues a job), /#/sync/{id}. Read API (no auth, JSONP allowed): GET https://mix-n-match.toolforge.org/api.php?query=... — catalogs (all 5,872, ~18MB), catalog_details&catalog=N (per-user match counts, month histogram), get_catalog_info, get_entry, get_entry_by_extid, search, entries_query, get_sync&catalog=N (conflict buckets, live-tested on #6372), rc / rc_atom (recent matching activity feed), download&catalog=N (full TSV: Q, ID, URL, Name, User — includes confirming username), download2 (TSV/JSON, column flags exturl/username/dates, row filters any_matched/firmly_matched/user_matched/unmatched/automatched, paged limit+offset). Write API (Wikimedia OAuth 1.0a session cookie required): match_q, match_q_multi, remove_q, import_source, upload_import_file (multipart, 512MB body cap), POST /api/v1/import_catalog. Rate limits: POSTs per-IP token bucket 30 burst / 10 per sec; 256 concurrent api.php requests then 503; sync's SPARQL result cached 300s; WDQS outage degrades get_sync gracefully (wd_unavailable banner, comparison buckets suppressed). Governance: any Wikimedia-account holder can log in (OAuth) and create catalogs; catalog owner or site admin can edit_catalog; the import UI warns when the property is already claimed by another catalog (one-catalog-per-property norm); catalogs are deactivated (active=0), not user-deletable.
reports.existing_crosslinks: None for Mathlib: no catalog matches "mathlib"/"lean" among all 5,872 (checked full api.php?query=catalogs dump). Ecosystem precedents already live: Metamath statement #6372 (wd_prop=12888 — direct formal-library analog; its live get_sync shows 49 wd_no_mm theorems like Q192760/fta, Q11518/pythag with P12888 set on Wikidata but unconfirmed in the catalog, 0 conflicts), nLab ID #2261, ProofWiki #2780/2781/2782, MathWorld #3523, Encyclopedia of Mathematics #1502/#2939, OEIS #1503, zbMATH author #2146. On the Wikidata side, P14534 "Mathlib Declaration ID" exists with formatter URL https://leanprover-community.github.io/mathlib4_docs/find/?pattern=$1#doc and exactly 4 uses (Q11567, Q863912, Q192760, Q179208 — note Q192760 and Q179208 already carry Metamath IDs too, a ready-made three-way consistency probe).
reports.quality_mechanism: Layered, auditable, and human-attributed. (1) Confirming a match in the SPA does two writes: match_q into the MnM DB attributed to the named Wikimedia user, AND an immediate wbeditentity on Wikidata executed AS THAT USER (their contributions), with a canonical reference block (P248 stated-in catalog source item, reference-URL, P813 retrieved — Codeberg #49 fix, src/api/data.rs prep_match_claim) and edit summary "Matched to [[:toollabs:mix-n-match/#/entry/{id}|{name} (#{id})]] in [[:toollabs:mix-n-match/#/catalog/{cid}|{catalog}]]" — every statement in WD history links back to its source row. A wbgetclaims pre-check prevents duplicate statements. (2) A background bot (wd_match_sync, OAuth bot account) pushes P{prop}=ext_id only for FULLY matched entries (user>0 AND q>0) on active catalogs with wd_prop and no wd_qual, re-verifies live Wikidata immediately before each write, and on divergence marks the row MULTIPLE/DIFFERENT and raises an issue for human review — "we don't try to 'fix' it". Summary: "Mix'n'match entry [[:toollabs:mix-n-match/#/entry/{id}|#{id}]]". (3) get_sync exposes conflict buckets per catalog: different (MnM says Qa, WD says Qb), mm_dupes, mm_double, wd_no_mm, mm_no_wd. (4) microsync job (4h budget) writes a human-readable discrepancy report to "User:Magnus Manske/Mix'n'match report/{catalog_id}" and auto-marks entries fully matched (user 4) when Wikidata already carries the statement. (5) Import hardening: fully-matched entries never deleted or overwritten on refresh; JSON import can't attribute matches to arbitrary users; removing a match in MnM does NOT auto-remove the WD statement (deliberate friction). (6) Anti-abuse: OAuth-gated mutations, per-IP POST rate limiting, origin checks, JSONP blocked on all mutating queries.

linkage_opportunities.to: New Mix'n'match catalog 'Mathlib declaration' bound to P14534 (the community-validation channel for the 815)
linkage_opportunities.join_key: ext_id = Mathlib declaration name (the P14534 value); q = Wikidata QID from WikiLean's QID-keyed catalog
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Create via https://mix-n-match.toolforge.org/#/import (any Wikimedia OAuth account; property field=14534 — import UI warns if already claimed). Import as MetaEntry JSONL via POST /api/v1/import_catalog: the 126-mapping gold seed as {q, user:<Jack's uid>} = fully matched; the 815 AI mappings as {q, user:0} = preliminary automatches that the community confirms in Match mode/game mode. ext_url can be omitted (auto-built from P14534's formatter URL) or set to WikiLean's /decl/:name resolver; ext_desc = statement of the theorem/def (≤255 chars) so matchers can judge without leaving the page.
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: CRITICAL: do NOT use the TSV q/autoq columns for the 815 — current code (meta_entry.rs) stamps BOTH as user=4 (fully matched), and fully-matched entries are what the wd_match_sync bot pushes to Wikidata as statements; that would auto-publish 815 unreviewed AI mappings. Use JSON user:0, then verify with api.php?query=get_entry_by_extid that test entries land as preliminary (user=0) before bulk import; pre-import, every ext_id must resolve against Mathlib (WikiLean /decl/:name 200, or mathlib4_docs find) and every QID must round-trip to the expected enwiki article.

linkage_opportunities.to: Wikidata P14534 statements (the actual payoff edits)
linkage_opportunities.join_key: (QID, Mathlib decl name) pair
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Two write paths, both automatic: (a) each community confirm fires an immediate wbeditentity as that user with the canonical reference block and an edit summary linking the MnM entry; (b) the MnM bot's wd_match_sync sweeps fully-matched entries whose item lacks P14534 (WD_MISSING) and wbcreateclaim's them — so importing the 126 gold as fully-matched seeds ~122 new P14534 statements without further action.
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Bot re-verifies live Wikidata immediately before each write and skips divergent rows (marks MULTIPLE + issue, never overwrites); every statement is traceable via its edit summary to a MnM entry and a named confirmer; duplicate-claim guard via wbgetclaims; P14534's Wikidata property constraints (format/distinct-values) provide an independent violation report.

linkage_opportunities.to: WikiLean catalog/data/*.jsonl harvest-back (promote AI mappings to community-reviewed)
linkage_opportunities.join_key: ext_id ↔ decl name; q ↔ QID in WikiLean's QID-keyed mapping catalog
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Nightly ops job polls api.php?query=download&catalog=<id> (TSV includes the confirming User per row) and query=rc for event stream; any row where user is a real human account and q equals WikiLean's stored QID → mark that mapping community-validated in catalog/data; q differing from WikiLean's → route to Jack's review queue.
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Never auto-accept a differing q: cross-check against WikiLean's annotation for that decl and the get_sync 'different' bucket; downloads are read-only/no-auth so the harvest cannot mutate MnM; provenance rule mirrors WikiLean's own — a human MnM confirmation upgrades provenance, a user=0/user=4 row does not.

linkage_opportunities.to: Mix'n'match sync page + microsync (drift detection vs the @[wikidata] mathlib pipeline)
linkage_opportunities.join_key: P14534 external-ID values on Wikidata vs catalog matches
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: get_sync&catalog=<id> returns wd_no_mm (P14534 statements created outside MnM — e.g. future statements derived from merged @[wikidata] tags — not yet reflected in the catalog; one click 'import from Wikidata' runs sync_match_q_multi, attributed to bot user 4) and mm_no_wd (confirmed matches awaiting bot push); microsync additionally publishes a persistent discrepancy report page.
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: Sync comparisons are WDQS-sourced with a 300s cache and are suppressed entirely when WDQS is down (wd_unavailable flag) so an outage can't masquerade as mass mm_no_wd; conflicts land in the 'different' bucket for humans, never auto-resolved.

linkage_opportunities.to: Auto-updating catalog from a WikiLean-published export (keep MnM in sync with Mathlib master)
linkage_opportunities.join_key: source_url of the catalog's update_info record
linkage_opportunities.mechanism: Publish a stable TSV/JSONL export from the WikiLean worker (e.g. wikilean.jackmccarthy.org/mnm-catalog.tsv, regenerated by the nightly from D1/catalog data as Mathlib decls get added/renamed); set it as the catalog's source URL so the update_from_tabbed_file job (8h budget) re-imports on demand — new decls appear, renamed decls update.
linkage_opportunities.correctness_check: add_replace mode never deletes; even add_replace_delete never touches fully-matched entries, and re-imports preserve existing q/user/timestamp on fully-matched rows — community verdicts are structurally protected from being clobbered by WikiLean's own refreshes.

wikilean_hooks: catalog/data/*.jsonl is already the exact shape MnM wants: QID + decl name + provenance → a ~40-line generator script emits MetaEntry JSONL (gold seed user=<uid>, AI rows user=0)
The live GET /decl/:name resolver (wiki/src) is the per-entry URL and the pre-import anti-hallucination gate: any ext_id that 404s is slop and must not ship

site/ops nightly is the natural home for the harvest-back poller (query=download + query=rc) and for regenerating the auto-update export; manage/status.py can surface 'MnM confirmations pending review' counts
The @[wikidata] bot pipeline and the MnM catalog both ultimately emit P14534 statements — get_sync's wd_no_mm/different buckets become a free cross-pipeline consistency check
P14534's formatter URL (mathlib4_docs find?pattern=$1) means entry URLs work with zero per-entry effort even before WikiLean URLs are used
Q192760 + Q179208 carry both P14534 and Metamath P12888 — seed rows for a cross-formal-library consistency probe WikiLean could own
key_facts: No Mathlib/Lean catalog exists in Mix'n'match (all 5,872 catalogs scanned via api.php?query=catalogs, 2026-07-02); P14534 is unclaimed by any catalog

Direct precedent: 'Metamath statement' catalog #6372 (https://mix-n-match.toolforge.org/#/catalog/6372) — P12888, 1,295 entries, created via plain import by ordinary user Uniwah (July 2024), 388 human-confirmed + 278 auto + 629 unmatched; its live get_sync shows 49 famous theorems (fta, pythag, ax-ac…) with P12888 on Wikidata awaiting catalog sync and zero conflicts

Import spec (meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Mix'n'match/Import): TSV/CSV, header row, id+name mandatory; optional desc/url/type/q/autoq/born/died/Pxxx; 1000-row test then queued job; OR MetaEntry JSON/JSONL via POST /api/v1/import_catalog (modes add_replace / add_replace_delete; fully-matched entries never deleted; response reports created/updated/skipped_fully_matched)

Confirming a match writes Wikidata IMMEDIATELY as the confirming user (wbeditentity via their OAuth session) with references P248+reference-URL+P813-retrieved and summary 'Matched to [[:toollabs:mix-n-match/#/entry/{id}|…]] in [[:toollabs:mix-n-match/#/catalog/{cid}|…]]'; a separate bot job (wd_match_sync) pushes statements for fully-matched entries that lack them, edit summary 'Mix'n'match entry [[:toollabs:mix-n-match/#/entry/{id}|#{id}]]', re-verifying live WD before each write

Conflict policy: catalog-says-A/Wikidata-says-B is NEVER auto-fixed — rows get status DIFFERENT/MULTIPLE + an issue for human review; get_sync exposes buckets {different, mm_dupes, mm_double, wd_no_mm, mm_no_wd}; microsync writes a report page under User:Magnus Manske/Mix'n'match report/{catalog_id} and auto-fully-matches (user 4) entries whose statement already exists on WD

LOADED FOOTGUN for the 815: current deployed code (src/meta_entry.rs) treats BOTH 'q' and 'autoq' TSV columns as user=4 = fully matched = bot will push to Wikidata; only the JSON import path with user:0 yields true preliminary matches — and the JSON path blocks attributing matches to any user other than the importer

Governance: any Wikimedia OAuth account can create catalogs (mix-n-match.toolforge.org/#/import); owner or admin edits catalog metadata; import UI warns when a property already has a catalog; catalogs are deactivated, not deleted; per-IP POST rate limit 30-burst/10-per-sec, 512MB upload cap, 256-concurrent-request 503 cap

Scale norms: catalogs range from ~100 rows (ProofWiki axioms) to 1,009,918 (zbMATH author); a 1,400-entry Mathlib catalog is small-normal; auto-refresh from a source_url is supported (update_from_tabbed_file job)

reports.source: Mix'n'match case studies + Mathlib catalog playbook — primary sources: mix-n-match.toolforge.org API (catalog_overview/get_catalog_info/catalog_details/download2/get_sync, verified live 2026-07-02), meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Mix'n'match/Import + /Manual + Talk:Mix'n'match, wikidata.org (Property:P12888, Property:P14534, Wikidata:Property_proposal/Metamath_statement_label, Wikidata:Property_proposal/Mathlib_declaration_ID, Wikidata:Database_reports/Constraint_violations/P14534, SPARQL counts), github.com/magnusmanske/mixnmatch_rs source
reports.what_it_is: Case-study evidence on how math/science Mix'n'match catalogs actually run, plus a concrete import recipe for WikiLean's 815 AI + 126 gold Wikidata→Mathlib mappings. Verified: catalog #6372 IS "Metamath statement" (bound to P12888, owner Uniwah uid 12546678, note "Created via import", type science). Its lifecycle: property proposed by Uniwah 2024-06-21 (page title is "Metamath statement label"), P12888 created 2024-07-10 by ZI Jony, catalog imported same month with 1,295 entries (curated df-* definitions subset of set.mm's 45,272 statements, exactly the phased plan stated in the proposal), 384 matches confirmed in July 2024 — 379 of them by the owner himself — 4 more in Sept 2024, then dormant 22 months. Today: 388 fully matched (30%), 278 preliminary, 629 unmatched, 0 N/A; 438 live P12888 statements. Comparison catalogs: MathWorld #3523 (15,347 entries, 7,626 matched ≈50% — the success case, driven by 2 power users doing 87% and a deliberately scoped import), nLab #2261 (13,752 entries, only 1,115 confirmed vs 9,209 preliminary automatches rotting since a 2019 scraper import), OEIS #1503 (299,999 entries, 442 matched = 0.15%, orphan), ProofWiki #2780/2781/2782 and Encyclopedia-of-Mathematics #1502 (imported with NO property bound — matches write nothing back; ProofWiki's 2,556 P6781 statements came from elsewhere). No Mathlib or Lean catalog exists yet (search_catalogs verified) — the field is clear for WikiLean.
reports.data_model: Catalog record: id, name, desc, wd_prop (Wikidata property number; nullable — the fatal ProofWiki mistake), type (e.g. "science"), search_wp (e.g. "en"), owner uid, active flag, types filter, do_auto_update, optional autoscraper JSON. Entry record (download2 fields): entry_id (internal), external_id (the identifier written to Wikidata — for WikiLean: the Mathlib decl name), name (label the automatcher and human matchers see — MUST be the Wikipedia title, not the decl name), description (hard-truncated at 255 chars; Metamath's raw ASCII "|- ( ph /\\ ps ...)" descriptions truncate mid-word), external_url, entry_type, q, mnm_user_id, matched_on timestamp. Match states: unmatched (noq), preliminarily matched (autoq — internal only, never written to Wikidata), fully matched (manual — written immediately), multi_match (several automatch candidates), N/A (not-in-Wikidata tombstone). Import file (meta:Mix'n'match/Import): CSV or TSV, single header row; mandatory columns id + name; optional desc, url (auto-generated from the property formatter if omitted), type (Qxxx), born/died, autoq ("Qxxx for educated guesses" → preliminary), q ("Qxxx for confirmed matches" → fully matched at import), Pxxx auxiliary-property columns (WARNING: these feed the Auxiliary data matcher bot, uid 4, which auto-CONFIRMS without human review — 313 nLab / 343 MathWorld / 4 Metamath matches).
reports.access: Tool: https://mix-n-match.toolforge.org/#/catalog/6372 (SPA). Machine-readable API (no auth, verified with curl): api.php?query=get_catalog_info&catalog=6372 (metadata), ?query=catalog_overview&catalogs=6372,2261,3523 (match stats: total/manual/autoq/multi_match/noq/na), ?query=catalog_details&catalog=N (per-user + per-month activity), ?query=download2&catalogs=N&format=json&limit=N&columns={"q":1} (full entry dump incl. matches, user ids, timestamps — the harvest channel), ?query=get_sync&catalog=N (bidirectional Wikidata diff: different/mm_double/mm_dupes/mm_no_wd/wd_no_mm), ?query=search_catalogs&q=... . Import UI: https://mix-n-match.toolforge.org/#/import — requires Wikimedia OAuth login (Jack's Wikidata account "Mynus grey" works); file upload, pasted data, or import-from-URL (Google Sheets publish-to-web supported for auto-updating catalogs). Docs: https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Mix'n'match/Import and /Manual. Source: github.com/magnusmanske/mixnmatch_rs (api/router.rs lists all endpoints). Constraint reports: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Wikidata:Database_reports/Constraint_violations/P14534 (regenerated daily by KrBot).
reports.existing_crosslinks: Property↔catalog binding is via P2264 (Mix'n'match catalog ID) on the property page: P4215 nLab→2261, P2812 MathWorld→3523 (both verified in entity JSON). P12888 carries no P2264 statement even though catalog 6372 is bound tool-side (wd_prop=12888) — add P2264 for discoverability, Metamath forgot. P14534 has no catalog and no P2264 yet; it does carry P1659 (related property) → P12888, P6781, P4215, P2812, formatter URL https://leanprover-community.github.io/mathlib4_docs/find/?pattern=$1#doc, and P1896 source repo. Statement counts today (SPARQL): P12888=438, P14534=4, P2812=6,763, P4215=4,329, P7554=1,212, P6781=2,556, P829=444. Flow-back is real and near-lossless where a property is bound: Metamath catalog 388 confirmed matches ↔ 438 statements (≈50 famous theorems — fta, birthday, pnt, ramsey, cayleyhamilton — were added directly on Wikidata outside the definitions-only catalog; get_sync wd_no_mm lists them), with exactly 1 write failure (df-oc in mm_no_wd).
reports.quality_mechanism: Layered, and the load-bearing layer is the preliminary/confirmed gate: autoq matches are internal to the tool and write NOTHING to Wikidata until a logged-in human clicks Confirm. Empirically verified: Metamath's automatch pile contains grotesque junk — df-ac→Q34433 (University of Oxford), df-gru→Q572146 (Dmitri Polyakov, Soviet spy), df-mi→Q667729 (Wildendürnbach, Austrian village), all timestamped 2024-07-18, userless — and none of those items has a P12888 statement today. The gate held. Once confirmed, the write is immediate and attributed ("Whenever you make a connection... the system will automatically update Wikidata"), but "removing a match (currently) does not" propagate — mistakes need manual Wikidata cleanup. Post-write QA: KrBot's daily constraint-violations report (already caught prime ideal Q863912 on P14534 two days after creation) and the get_sync diff report (different / mm_double = several entries on one item / mm_no_wd / wd_no_mm). Owner tools: "purge automatches" for a polluted preliminary pile. Norms (manual): "Don't guess", "Check the automatic matches", don't match disambiguation items; good entry descriptions are explicitly cited as the main lever against wrong matches. Known criticisms (Talk:Mix'n'match): edit summaries name the tool but not the catalog; no bulk-delete for obsolete entries (a 10k+ request pending); catalog update knowledge dies with the creator; UI breakage Mar–May 2026; automatcher confuses similarly-named items and disambiguation pages. The Auxiliary data matcher bot bypasses the human gate when aux Pxxx columns exist — a slop vector to avoid for AI-generated data.

reports.what_it_is: The Stacks Project (stacks.math.columbia.edu) is an open-source collaborative textbook on algebraic geometry/algebraic stacks (~7,600 pages, 116 chapters) maintained since 2005 by Aise Johan de Jong (Columbia), with website infrastructure (Gerby: a plasTeX fork + Flask app) maintained by Pieter Belmans. Its defining asset for WikiLean is the tag system: 21,436 permanent 4-character identifiers, one per statement/section/equation, that never change and are never reused — the de-facto stable citation currency for algebraic geometry (nLab, Wikipedia, arXiv papers, and mathlib all cite tags). Kerodon (kerodon.net, Jacob Lurie's textbook on categorical homotopy theory, 8,189 tags) is 'modeled on the Stacks project' and runs the identical Gerby tag infrastructure. Content license: GNU Free Documentation License ('All contributions are licensed under the GNU Free Documentation License' — site footer); full LaTeX source public at github.com/stacks/stacks-project.
reports.data_model: A tag is a 4-char string over digits + capital letters (letter 'O' dropped after tag 04DO to avoid 0-confusion; alphabet gives 1,679,616 combinations). The canonical registry is ONE flat file: `tags/tags` in github.com/stacks/stacks-project (21,573 lines; 21,436 active tag lines + 137 comment lines). Line format: `tag,full_label` where full_label = `<chapter-tex-file>-<type>-<label>` — e.g. `01JF,schemes-section-representable`, `000C,sets-lemma-axiom-regularity`. So tag→(chapter, statement-type, LaTeX label) resolution is a single string split; statement type is embedded in the label. Breakdown by type (computed from the file): 12,581 lemma, 3,415 section, 1,721 definition, 1,041 remark, 685 equation, 449 example, 440 item, 386 exercise, 330 proposition, 215 theorem, 124 situation. Numeric references (e.g. 'Lemma 13.21.2') are NOT stable — they come from the compiled book and shift on reorganization; the /structure API and statement HTML expose the current tag↔reference mapping. New tags are assigned only by the maintainer 'every once in a while using a script' (first 3,026 tags minted 2009-05-16). Kerodon uses the same 4-char format but its tags file/source is not public.
reports.access:
Website + a documented but self-declared UNSTABLE API at stacks.math.columbia.edu/api, three endpoint families (all verified live 2026-07): (1) `GET /data/tag//structure` → JSON tree {tag, type, reference, name, children[]} — works on part/chapter-level tags (e.g. /data/tag/0ELT/structure returns Part 4 'Algebraic Spaces' down to individual lemmas with both tag and reference number); statement-level tags return 'This tag does not have a structure'; docs say to treat the 9 parts separately. (2) `GET /data/tag//content/statement` → HTML of the statement without proof, e.g. for 015I: `
…Lemma 13.21.2… There exists a Cartan-Eilenberg resolution…` (LaTeX inline, MathJax-ready — the env-* class and reference number are machine-parseable). (3) `GET /data/tag//content/full` → HTML with proof. Human pages at /tag/, citation helper at /tag//cite. API docs ask implementers to email stacks.project@gmail.com before building on it. Bulk access: the whole tags file + LaTeX source via GitHub raw (no rate concerns, GFDL). Kerodon exposes the SAME Gerby endpoints (verified: kerodon.net/data/tag/008R/content/statement returns `
…Definition 2.2.4.17…`), but has no public repo, no tags-file dump, and no license/copyright statement anywhere I could find on the site.
reports.existing_crosslinks: (a) Mathlib→Stacks: 515 `@[stacks TAG "comment"]` occurrences in the local mathlib4 checkout = 349 unique tags on 492 declarations (plus 9 real `@[kerodon]` tags, e.g. @[kerodon 008R] on lax-functor material) — same CrossRefAttribute machinery as @[wikidata]; docs at Mathlib.Tactic.StacksAttribute; a `#stacks_tags` command lists all tagged decls. (b) Stacks→Mathlib: NOT live — I fetched raw HTML of tag pages 00VM, 0052, 09HK (all mathlib-tagged; 00VM is cited 14× in mathlib) and found zero leanprover/mathlib/formalization strings. However the Lean Zulip archive (stream 113488, topic 'stacks tags') records an agreed plan from ~Nov 2024: Pieter Belmans engaged directly, proposing icons in the Stacks ToC + per-tag-page links to mathlib docs, fed by an hourly-cached JSON dictionary of tag→decl exported from mathlib. It has apparently stalled pre-ship. (c) nLab cites Stacks tags pervasively and has dedicated `[tag:044Q]` link syntax; its citation guidance prefers tags over chapter numbers. (d) Wikipedia articles cite tag URLs in references (Nagata ring, Algebraic stack, Constructible set, Glossary of category theory…) but there is no Template:Stacks_Project (404). (e) Wikidata: the Stacks Project is item Q25099801, but SPARQL shows ZERO items using it via P1343 (described by source) or P248 (stated in) references, and NO property has a formatter URL containing stacks.math or kerodon — the structured-data field is completely open.
reports.quality_mechanism: Single-gatekeeper moderation: de Jong personally reviews and accepts every contribution; tags are minted only by him via script against the tags file, so the registry can't fork. Stability is contractual: 'The tag system provides stable references… its tag always keeps pointing to it,' and 'If it ever turns out that a lemma, theorem, etc. was wrong then we may remove it from the project. However, we will keep the tag, and there will be an explanation for its disappearance.' Tombstoning is concretely visible in tags/tags: 137 comment lines like `#06KV,formal-defos-section-presentations` preceded by '#disappeared in reorg chapter on formal deformation theory'; superseded-but-not-wrong results are moved to an 'obsolete' chapter with tags still resolving (e.g. 007V,obsolete-lemma-characterize-epi-mono). Per-tag comment threads on the site add community review (tag 01JF carries 7 comments). This never-reuse + tombstone-with-explanation policy is stronger than Wikipedia revids and makes Stacks tags close to ideal external-id material.

Then the P14534 rule: Jack human-reviews every mapping before submission. Permanence policy means links never rot.

reports.what_it_is: Google's proprietary entity database behind knowledge panels — launched May 16, 2012 (Amit Singhal's "things, not strings" post), seeded by Freebase (Metaweb, acquired 16 July 2010). Scale: 570M entities / 18B facts by Dec 2012 → 70B facts mid-2016 → 5B entities / 500B facts (May 2020). It is BOTH the architecture prior-art for entity-centric knowledge (schema.org typing, sameAs-style reconciliation, confidence-scored entity resolution) AND the anti-slop cautionary tale: no public documentation of implementation, unattributed knowledge panels (Taraborelli/WMF: it "undermines people's ability to verify information"), error incidents (Kannada "ugliest language" apology, June 2021), and measured Wikipedia readership decline from uncredited reuse. Its Freebase substrate died: announced 16 Dec 2014, shut 2 May 2016; the promised Wikidata migration largely failed — of ~14M statements loaded into the Primary Sources Tool, only ~90k approval/rejection actions by ~100 users had occurred by Jan 2016 (Tanon et al., "From Freebase to Wikidata: The Great Migration", WWW 2016). The durable public bridge is Wikidata: P2671 "Google Knowledge Graph ID" (8,166,257 uses, /g/-form only) plus P646 "Freebase ID" (4,469,823 uses, /m/-form — still valid kgmids in Google's API).
reports.data_model: Entities keyed by kgmid — two heritage forms: /m/xxxxx (Freebase-era, e.g. Pythagorean theorem = /m/05r2j) and /g/1... (Google-native, e.g. sine = /g/120qdg8r, topos = /g/128dgz813). Wikidata splits them: P2671 holds ONLY /g/ IDs (format regex `/g/1(?:[1258dhjnpqrstvwxyz][0-9b-df-hj-np-tv-z_]{6,8}|3b[013-9b-df-hj-np-tv-z_]{4}|3z[2356gmpqwz_]{6})`, single-value + distinct-values constraints, formatter URLs `https://www.google.com/search?kgmid=$1` and `https://g.co/kg$1`); /m/ IDs live in P646 (mass-imported from the 2013-10-28 Freebase dump, referenced per-statement). API entities are schema.org-typed (Thing/Person/etc.) JSON-LD: response = ItemList → itemListElement[] → EntitySearchResult { result: {@id: "kg:/m/05r2j", name, @type[], description, url, image, detailedDescription: {articleBody, url, license}}, resultScore }. detailedDescription snippets are Wikipedia text with an explicit CC BY-SA license URL. The API returns single entities only — NO relationship/edge export; the graph structure itself is never published.
reports.access: Knowledge Graph Search API: GET https://kgsearch.googleapis.com/v1/entities:search with params query | ids (repeatable: ?ids=/m/05r2j&ids=/g/120qdg8r) | languages | types (schema.org, OR-logic) | prefix | limit (default 20, max 500) | indent | key. API-key auth required (verified live: unauthenticated call returns 403 PERMISSION_DENIED "Method doesn't allow unregistered callers"). Free quota 100,000 read calls/day/project. Docs explicitly say it is read-only and "not suitable for use as a production-critical service"; Google is migrating it to Cloud Enterprise Knowledge Graph (Pre-GA terms, "same functionality"). CRITICAL ToS constraint (Google APIs ToS §5.e): you may not "scrape, build databases, or otherwise create permanent copies of such content, or keep cached copies longer than permitted by the cache header" — so bulk-harvesting and republishing API payloads is prohibited; §6.b requires displayed attribution. The safe path: obtain kgmids from Wikidata (P2671/P646, CC0 — freely storable/republishable) and use the API only for spot-check lookups; the detailedDescription Wikipedia snippets are independently CC BY-SA. No public dumps of the KG exist; Freebase's final dump (CC-BY) is the only historical artifact.
reports.existing_crosslinks: Wikidata is the entire public crosslink fabric: P2671 (8.17M uses) and P646 (4.47M uses) bridge QIDs → kgmids. Math coverage measured live via SPARQL (2026-07-02): 1,116 items carry both P2671 and P2812 (MathWorld ID) — e.g. sine Q152415 = /g/120qdg8r/"Sine", topos Q2143621 = /g/128dgz813/"Topos", number 2 Q200 = /g/1238gg37; 342 instance-of mathematical concept (Q24034552) items have P2671; 806 theorem-class items (P31/P279* Q65943) have P2671. Big theorems often have only the /m/ form: Pythagorean theorem Q11518 has NO P2671 but P646 = /m/05r2j. Against WikiLean's own concept graph (catalog/data/concept_graph.json, 1,376 QIDs): 74 nodes have P2671, 1,312 have P646, 1,348 (98.0%) have at least one kgmid, only 28 have neither. WikiLean's 4 live P14534 (Mathlib declaration ID) items — complex number/Complex, Cayley's theorem, fundamental theorem of algebra, prime ideal — currently have zero P2671 overlap, so the join to Google runs through P646 for now. Google itself consumes Wikipedia/Wikidata as sources (one-directional, uncredited) — the KG links IN but publishes no links OUT.
reports.quality_mechanism: The anti-model. Quality control is opaque algorithmic merging with no public provenance: no documentation of implementation, no per-fact sourcing, no edit interface beyond a "Feedback" link, no revision history. Consequences on record: unattributed knowledge panels (the Betty White example), Wikimedia's Taraborelli calling it a verifiability regression, the 2021 Kannada incident, and error propagation FROM Wikipedia INTO panels with no way to trace or patch upstream. The API exposes only resultScore (an unexplained relevance/confidence number) — useful as a candidate-ranking pattern, useless as provenance. Contrast Freebase (open curation, CC-BY, dumps) which Google closed and killed; and contrast the migration's human-review bottleneck (Primary Sources Tool: 14M statements staged, ~90k human decisions) — evidence that dumping AI/bulk statements on a volunteer review queue without ranking or provenance stalls. WikiLean's design (D1-canonical, provenance:"human" floor with 422 protection, tombstones, propose-then-approve) is already the corrective architecture; the KG's lesson is what happens without it.
